title: Quick TrustRadius note request — EdgeHealth pilot
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Priya Shah <priya@edgehealth.com>
Date: Sat, 30 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Quick TrustRadius note request — EdgeHealth pilot

Hi Priya,

Thanks again for running the two-week pilot with our low-latency endpoint — really appreciate the close collaboration from you and the ops team. If you had a couple of minutes, would you be open to posting a short note on TrustRadius about your experience? Short + specific is perfect (2–4 sentences). It helps other teams and we share these internally for product priorities.

We can send a $75 e-gift voucher as a thank-you. No need to mention Redwood in the compensation; it’s just a token of appreciation. I attached a one-page set of steps for TrustRadius and a tiny suggested template below to make it quick.

Suggested template (copy/paste friendly):
- "We piloted Redwood Inference for two weeks to evaluate latency and cost for inference. The integration was straightforward, and we saw ~30% lower per-token cost with comparable latency. Support was responsive during set up."

If you prefer, we can also add a single bullet about the KV cache behavior we enabled. If you're OK with this, I’ll send the link and the voucher.

Deadline request: if you could post by end of next week (June 5), that would be awesome but no pressure.

Thanks — let me know what name/title you want listed on the review and if ops should get the voucher link directly.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0123
[attachment: TrustRadius-review-steps.pdf (pdf, 180KB)]

From: Priya Shah <priya@edgehealth.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 30 May 2026 10:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick TrustRadius note request — EdgeHealth pilot

Alex,

Thanks — happy to do that. Couple quick Qs before I post:
1) Would you prefer the review to be attributed to me (Priya Shah, Director of Data Science) or to EdgeHealth as a company account?
2) Can you share a very short bulleted "impact statement" we can borrow (1–2 bullets) — helps our comms team when we post.
3) How will the e-gift voucher be delivered? Invoice or code?

If you send the link and the short bullets, I can get it live early next week.

Thanks,
Priya
Director of Data Science, EdgeHealth
priya@edgehealth.com

Also: Ben, any objection to including one-line metric about latency/cost (we measured ~30% lower per-token cost during pilot) — I want to make sure we're comfortable sharing that publicly.

My draft impact bullets to send to Priya:
- "Reduced inference unit cost by ~30% compared to their prior setup during the pilot window (2-week sample)."
- "Integration required a single engineer day and used prefix/KV caching to preserve latency."

Thanks,
Alex

title: Guest Speaker Fees + Event Card Spend Limits
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: hello@mayaevents.com, priya@uni.edu
Date: Mon, 25 May 2026 14:22:00 -0700
Subject: Guest speaker fee + pre-authorization for on-site card

Hi team,

We have confirmed Dr. Priya Rao as the keynote for our June 18th Bay Area user event (Maya Events is handling on-site ops). Maya attached the signed SOW and an invoice for the speaker fee. Two asks so we can close logistics: 

1) PO + cost center coding for the speaker fee (invoice attached).
2) Temporary spend limit on the Redwood event card for day-of vendor incidentals — Maya is asking for a $2,500 pre-authorization that they can use for last-minute AV rentals and hospitality. We'll reconcile actuals against receipts.

Quick context: total speaker fee on the invoice is $5,000 (one-time), Maya expects to bill for incidentals on the event card if approved. We prefer to have the PO in place before payments go out to match procurement rules.

Attachments: Maya_Events_SOW.pdf, Speaker_Invoice_PriyaRao_signed.pdf

Next step: Laura/Ben, can you confirm PO number and cost center? Ben — if you can provision a temporary card limit and note any controls we should apply (e.g., MCC restrictions), that would help Maya.

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Field Marketing Manager
Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 26 May 2026 09:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Guest speaker fee + pre-authorization for on-site card

Alex — thanks for the details and attachments. A few items we need before we can issue PO and enable a card limit: 

- Confirm vendor legal name and W-9 (if US vendor) or equivalent tax docs. Is Maya Events registered as "Maya Events Ltd." in the US?
- Clarify whether Dr. Priya Rao is invoicing as an individual (1099) or through a third-party institution — we need payee entity for tax reporting.
- Confirm cost center and GL code to charge the speaker fee (default would be 5002-Events-Marketing unless you'd like a different bucket).

Assuming the invoice is correct, I can approve a PO for the $5,000 speaker fee. For the on-site card pre-auth, finance prefers to set a $2,500 single-day cap and enable MCC filtering to prevent cash advances. Ben — can you confirm we can add those restrictions and how long the limit can remain active?

If we get the W-9 and payee confirmation today, I can route PO creation and have procurement issue PO_EVT-3427 by EOD tomorrow.

Thanks,
Laura Bennett
Senior Finance Manager — Revenue & Ops
Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai


From: Maya Events Ltd. <hello@mayaevents.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: priya@uni.edu
Date: Wed, 27 May 2026 11:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Guest speaker fee + pre-authorization for on-site card

Hi Alex / Laura,

Thanks — attached are the signed SOW and a scanned invoice for Dr. Priya Rao (invoice indicates the speaker is billing through her consulting LLC: \"Priya Rao Consulting, LLC\"). W-9 for Priya is attached to the invoice bundle. Vendor legal name on our side is \"Maya Events Ltd.\" (US EIN on file).

We can accept a PO for the $5,000 speaker fee (Net 30). Regarding the event card, we request a $2,500 pre-authorization for the day-of incidentals (AV contingency, last-minute rentals, site toiletries). We will provide receipts within 48 hours post-event and return any unused balance to Redwood.

Attachments: Maya_Events_SOW.pdf, Speaker_Invoice_PriyaRao_signed.pdf

Please let us know if you need a separate vendor registration link filled out — happy to complete.

Best,
Nina Patel
Operations Lead, Maya Events Ltd.
hello@mayaevents.com | +1 (415) 555-2076


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.ai>, hello@mayaevents.com
Date: Sat, 30 May 2026 11:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Guest speaker fee + pre-authorization for on-site card

All — approved. Summary of actions taken and instructions: 

- PO created: PO_EVT-3427 (procurement link: https://procurement.redwood.internal/po/EVT-3427). Sent to Maya Events; copy in Drive folder.
- Cost coding: 5002-Events-Marketing / CC: 789-06. Charge class: External Events — FY26.
- Payment terms: Speaker fee $5,000 via PO (Net 30). Priya Rao Consulting, LLC is the payee. Please route the signed invoice to procurement@redwood.ai for processing.
- Event card: temporary single-day spend limit set to $2,500 on 2026-06-18 (12:00 AM–11:59 PM PT). MCC restrictions applied: no cash advances (6011), no gambling merchant categories. Card controls will expire automatically after 24 hours.

Notes/next steps for Alex: Confirm Maya received the PO, and confirm Priya's invoice goes to procurement@redwood.ai. After the event, submit a reconciliation packet (receipts + expense summary) to finance within 72 hours so we can close the PO and reconcile the card pre-auth.

Approved by: Ben Carter
Director, Finance Operations
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

title: Redwood demo: organizer swap to preserve external join tokens
preview:
From: Samira Khan <samira.khan@finops.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 23 May 2026 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Redwood demo invite: join button greyed out

Hi Ava,

Thanks for scheduling. I accepted the invite but a bunch of folks in our org report the join button is greyed out or shows an expired token when launched from our shared customer calendar (FinOps Customer Shared). Our SSO strips external meeting tokens when events are created by a calendar resource.

Can you resend from a Redwood user account (so the organizer is a person, not the shared calendar) and include a Meet link with PST dial-in and conference ID? Procurement needs the dial-in in the event body (some clients don't surface attachments). If Zoom is created, we need the organizer to be an approved external partner, which I don't think you are on file for.

I'm copying procurement. We want to keep Monday 09:00 PT if possible.

Thanks,
Samira Khan
Senior IT Ops Manager, FinOps.io
Attachment: finops-demo-invite.ics (calendar stub)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira.khan@finops.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 23 May 2026 16:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Redwood demo invite: join button greyed out

Hi Samira 14 thanks for the note and the context.

That matches a few support cases we've seen. Plan:
- I'll recreate the event from my personal Redwood account so the organizer is an internal user (avoids token stripping).
- Include both a Google Meet URL (per your procurement preference) and a Redwood-hosted Zoom link as a fallback.
- Paste PST dial-in + conference ID into the event body (and the email body).
- Attach a fresh .ics and the demo agenda.

If procurement insists on Meet-only, I'll remove Zoom and keep Meet as the sole URL. Which do you prefer?

I'll resend tomorrow morning so it propagates before Monday.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
Attachment: redwood-demo-templates.zip (agenda + .ics templates)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: infra-scheduling@redwood.com
Date: Sun, 24 May 2026 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: FinOps shared calendar token strip (context)

Forwarding for awareness: FinOps shared calendar strips external join tokens when the organizer is a calendar resource. Quick playbook:
- Recreate events from the salesperson's user account and invite the shared calendar as an attendee. That preserves external join tokens.
- If customer policy requires Meet-only, ensure event is created by an internal user who has permission to create external Meet links.
- Always paste dial-in + conference ID in the event body (some mail clients or calendar syncs drop attachments).

I'll have Ava resend with these constraints applied.

From: Samira Khan <samira.khan@finops.io>

Cc: procurement@finops.io
Date: Sun, 24 May 2026 11:46:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Redwood demo invite: join button greyed out

Ava 14 a couple of procurement constraints to confirm before you resend:
- Prefer Meet links for external demos for audit logging; Zoom allowed only if organizer is an approved external partner.
- Dial-in and conference ID must be pasted in the event body (attachments not reliable).
- Do not create the event from FinOps Customer Shared; create from your internal user and invite that shared calendar as an attendee if you want it on the shared calendar.

Procurement also wants the demo recording uploaded to a shared Drive after the session; I can provide the folder if needed.

Samira
Attachment: procurement-rules.pdf (summary)

Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, procurement@finops.io
Date: Mon, 25 May 2026 09:17:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Redwood demo invite: join button greyed out

Hi Samira 14 invite resent from my account. Details pasted below and attached. Please confirm receipt.

Event: Redwood platform demo for FinOps.io
When: Mon, 09:00 PT
Organizer: Ava Chen (ava.chen@redwood.com)
Google Meet: https://meet.google.com/abc-defg-hij
Dial-in (US PST): +1 669-555-0110, PIN: 4321#
Zoom (fallback): https://redwood.zoom.us/j/123456789 (recording enabled)
Agenda: quick intro, demo (30 min), Q&A (15 min)
I've attached redwood-demo.ics and demo-agenda.pdf. I'll upload the recording to the Drive folder after the session.

If anyone still sees a greyed join button, let me know and I'll send a direct link to the group thread.

Thanks,
Ava

text:
From: Samira Khan <samira.khan@finops.io>

title: Velori — request for trial top-up + credits (exec approval)
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Tom Richards <tom.richards@velori.ai>, Sara Liu <sara@velori.ai>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Velori — request for trial top-up + credits (exec approval)

Hi Tom / Sara,

Thanks again for taking the demo last week — loved seeing the billing-reconciliation use case you built on top of Redwood. You mentioned the integration test run next week is the gating factor for a short pilot decision. To make that smoother, I wanted to propose a limited onramp sweetener so you can run the end-to-end tests and some traffic without hitting hard limits.

Proposal (what I'm authorized to offer today):
- 100,000 free token credits applied to your account (usable across models)
- 30-day trial extension from your current expiry date
- A dedicated onboarding session with our solutions engineer (2 hours)

If that isn't sufficient to complete the integration testing, I can escalate for executive approval for an expanded package. To move quickly: can you send over a short list of the integration tests you need and an estimated token burn? Also attached is a quick checklist we use for onramp success.

If you're available, I can put 15 minutes on the calendar to align next steps.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex@redwood.com | (415) 555-0124
Redwood Inference

Attachment: velori_onramp_checklist.pdf (attachment stub)


From: Tom Richards <tom.richards@velori.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 11:10:42 -0700
Subject: Re: Velori — request for trial top-up + credits (exec approval)

Alex — thanks, appreciate the quick reply and the 100k credit offer. A couple things we ran into while re-playing the tests:

- Our integration stress runs (including synthetic traffic and data transforms) will likely burn ~220k tokens over the next 3 weeks.
- Procurement needs the pilot to show baseline SLA + cost telemetry; they want an extra 30 days after the tests to evaluate.
- If we can get a small promotional discount on pilot rates (first month only), it materially helps the CFO sign-off.

I’ve attached the recent usage snapshot so you can see our token profile. Would you be able to escalate for ~200k credits + a 45-day extension and a 20% pilot discount for month 1? That combination would let us run the tests and keep the program moving while procurement finishes their review.

Thanks — happy to jump on a quick call.
Tom
Tom Richards
Head of Product, Velori Analytics
tom.richards@velori.ai | +1-212-555-0168

From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 13:05:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Velori — request for trial top-up + credits (exec approval)

Marissa / Ben — forwarding Velori's ask. Highlights: customer requests ~200k additional free token credits, a 45-day trial extension, and a 20% promo on month 1. They supplied a usage snapshot (attached) showing a bursty profile — most traffic concentrated in a few long sequences.

Why I think it's worth approving (business case):
- Velori is already engaged with two product teams; if pilot succeeds this is a high-propensity expansion into a paid production integration (our AE forecast: $75k ARR in year 1).
- The cost delta for us at our typical quantization/batching will be modest relative to the ACV upside — credits and a small promo de-risk the procurement calendar.
- They are open to a short MSA addendum and ROI metric commitments (we can gate the discount on completed integration tests).

Recommended exception (what I propose we ask execs to approve):
- 150,000 token credits (immediate) + ability to grant another 50k after integration milestone
- 30-day extension now; conditional 15-day extension after usage review (effectively 45 total if milestone met)
- 15% pilot discount for month 1, conditioned on signed addendum with success metrics

Risks / controls:
- Condition credits / promo on signed addendum and a POC success checklist (we should use the attached onboarding checklist + a short integration plan).
- Enable per-account burn alerts at 70% and 90% so we don't overspend.

Can you confirm if this is approvable? If yes I’ll communicate to the customer and get the DocuSign addendum started. Attaching the usage report they sent and a proposed MAP (doc) for the addendum.

Thanks,
Alex

Attachment: velori_trial_usage_report.pdf, proposed_map.docx


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Velori — request for trial top-up + credits (exec approval)

Alex / Ben — thanks for the summary. Approve with conditions.

Approved package (executive sign-off):
- 150,000 token credits to be applied immediately
- 30-day extension to the trial start date; discretionary 15-day extension to be granted only after we validate the integration milestone (i.e., their tests run and we see the telemetry described)
- 15% promotional discount for month 1 of the pilot (not combinable with other promos)

Conditions and next steps:
1) Get a signed addendum (I’ll approve the language in the proposed_map.docx) that includes: defined success metrics, a data retention note, and a termination clause.
2) Set up automatic burn alerts at 70% and 90% of the credit pool; escrow the second 50k behind milestone delivery.
3) Legal and CS should be looped if there are any additional security/compliance asks from Velori.
4) Deadline: customer must sign addendum and confirm integration plan by 2026-06-15 or the credit extension will revert to 30 days only.

Ben — please action the DocuSign addendum, configure the burn alerts in the console, and confirm when credits are loaded. Alex — send the confirmation to Tom/Sara and outline the conditions clearly.

Marissa Cole
VP, Sales — MidMarket, Redwood Inference

title: Warm signup — a few quick fit questions before we lock a demo slot
preview: From: Ava Chen To: Sofia Garcia Cc: Ben Carter Date: 2026-05-14T09:12:00-07:00 Subject: Quick fit questions after your self-signup Hi Sofia — thanks for signing up and trying Redwood! I pulled your account and saw some early activity — would love to do a brief call to make sure a demo is a fit and to surface the right pilot advice. Before we schedule, two quick areas to help us tailor the session: 1) Primary use case: what are you most looking to build? (examples: live customer support summarization, search/embeddings, content generation for marketing, internal knowledge base) 2) Traffic: rough QPS or daily requests today and target after 3 months (ballpark is fine) 3) Timeline & priority: are you looking for a pilot in 2 weeks / 1 month / 2+ months? If you can send 2–3 short bullets on those, I’ll propose three demo slots that work for our CS/solutions folks. I can also include Ben (SDR) or Monica (Customer Success) depending on who you want on the call. Example slots (PT): Tue 5/18 10:00–10:30, Wed 5/19 14:00–14:30, Thu 5/20 09:00–09:30 — happy to adjust to your timezone. Thanks — Ava Ava Chen Account Executive, Redwood Inference redwood.com Attachments: none in this message From: Sofia Garcia To: Ava Chen Date: 2026-05-14T10:03:00-07:00 Subject: Re: Quick fit questions after your self-signup Hi Ava — thanks for reaching out. Happy to answer quickly: 1) Use case: customer support triage + short-response generation and root-cause extraction. We want to triage incoming tickets and produce short summaries to push to agents and also generate suggested replies for first-touch. 2) Traffic: currently ~40k incoming messages/month (~1.3k/day) but we expect to ramp quickly once we integrate. Goal is ~120k/day within 3 months for peak season (we know this is aggressive; looking for guidance on batching and cost options). 3) Timeline: we’d like a pilot in ~4 weeks if possible (team bandwidth is limited in the next 2 weeks). Preferences / notes: we are fine starting on hosted, but we’ll likely need a dedicated/private option later due to data retention policies. Our team is PST (based in San Diego). Please include any pre-demo info we should prepare. —Sofia Sofia Garcia Head of AI, LeanRetail sofia@leanretail.io From: Ava Chen To: Sofia Garcia Cc: Ben Carter Date: 2026-05-14T10:28:00-07:00 Subject: Re: Quick fit questions after your self-signup Thanks Sofia — super helpful. Based on that traffic profile and timeline we’ll route you to our solutions CS to make the pilot concrete (Monica handles pilot scoping; copying her on the next message). A few quick notes: - For 120k/day you’ll want to consider prefix caching and batching; we’ll share a cost/throughput sketch on the call. - Hosted is fine to start; Dedicated/Private is an option when you need retention/isolated capacity. Can you confirm whether your data retention requirements are strict (e.g., prohibit persistence outside your VPC) or flexible for the pilot? Also, can you attach any traffic estimate or representative sample (CSV/Excel) — that helps our sizing. I’m attaching a short traffic template (traffic_estimates.xlsx) you can fill; if you prefer a call first we can lock a slot. Proposed slots (PT) — pick one, or tell me a time that works for you: • Tue 5/18 10:00–10:30 PT • Wed 5/19 14:00–14:30 PT • Thu 5/20 09:00–09:30 PT If none of those work, I can propose morning windows next week. Once you pick, I’ll send a calendar invite and include Monica for pilot scoping. Best, Ava Account Executive, Redwood Inference Attachments: traffic_estimates.xlsx (spreadsheet) From: Sofia Garcia To: Ava Chen Cc: Ben Carter Date: 2026-05-18T08:17:00-07:00 Subject: Re: Quick fit questions after your self-signup Hi Ava — thanks, I filled out the traffic template and attached it. Quick update: the 5/18 slot conflicts with an internal planning meeting; would you be able to move to Wed 5/19 15:00 PT instead? Also please include Monica Patel (I’ll loop her) — she’s leading pilots on our side and will want to drive the technical checklist. Also, we will want to discuss SSO/SCIM and logging/audit retention — happy to cover that on the same call. Can you confirm whether your data retention requirements are strict (e.g., prohibit persistence outside your VPC) or flexible for the pilot? title: Follow-up: scope & budget confirmation — batching & concurrency assumptions
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Samir Rao <samir@NimbusHealth.com>
Cc: Elena Park <elena.park@nimbushealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 15 May 2026 14:12:00 -0700
Subject: Follow-up: scope & budget confirmation — batching & concurrency assumptions

Hi Samir / Elena —

Thanks again for a constructive discovery call today. Quick recap and the concrete asks we agreed to: actionable items first, then the assumptions we used so the econ model matches your expectations.

Action items (owner / ETA)
- Nimbus: confirm expected monthly token volume ranges and peak qps window (Samir / Tue 5/19)
- Redwood: provide a draft PoA (pricing + POC config) and batch/concurrency knobs for your workload (Ava / Mon 5/18) — attached draft proposal + token profile CSV
- Both: schedule a 30-min tech sync to validate KV/prefix reuse and latency SLOs (tentative Wed 5/20)

What we heard from your team during the call
- Core workload: conversational triage + summarization for clinical intake (short prompts, average response ~180 tokens).
- Expected steady-state traffic: 50–120 requests/sec (rps) with strong diurnal trough/peak (peak ~10:00–14:00 PT).
- Sample request composition: 70% single-turn generation (no long history), 30% multi-turn with small context reuse (~3 turns, ~350 token prefix average).
- Target unit-econ: $300–$500 per 1M tokens (goal is to land at or below $400/1M for production).

Assumptions I used for the draft pricing and POC config (please flag anything off):
- Average generated tokens per request: 180 tokens; average prompt tokens: 50 tokens => ~230 tokens total per request.
- Baseline qps at steady-state: 75 rps (avg), peak concurrency target 180 concurrent requests (short tail).
- Continuous batching: expect ~15–25ms batching windows on inference nodes with prefix reuse/KV cache hits for the 30% multi-turn.
- Typical batch sizes: 8–32 depending on instance and sequence length (we'll tune during POC).
- Latency SLO: p95 generation < 300ms.
- Month estimate: 50M–200M tokens (varies with pilot scale).

Because your target unit-econ is sub-$500/1M, we recommended the Dedicated pool (reserved capacity) with a mixing policy: primary dedicated for steady traffic + burst to shared hosted for spikes. That gets predictable cost while allowing you to meet the p95 latency target.

Please confirm the monthly token low/mid/high bands and the current procurement timeline so we can finalize the PoA.

Thanks —
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | (415) 555-0124

Attachment stubs: poa-proposal-draft.pdf (pricing + config), nimbus_poc_token_profile.csv (sample token histogram)


From: Samir Rao <samir@NimbusHealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Park <elena.park@nimbushealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 15 May 2026 17:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: scope & budget confirmation — batching & concurrency assumptions

Thanks Ava — useful recap. A few clarifications from our side:

1) Token volumes
- Pilot (month 1): ~12–18M tokens (we'll be routing ~10k–14k requests/day).
- Ramp (months 2–4): 40–75M tokens/month as usage spreads across clinics.
- Production target (6–12 months): 150–220M tokens/month if we onboard 60% of clinics.

2) Concurrency / qps profile
- Baseline rps: 20–30 rps across the fleet (global), but concentrated spikes to 110–180 rps during intake waves in the morning.
- Long tail: occasional bursts up to 300 rps for short periods (30–90s) during system-wide batch imports.

3) Latency tolerance
- Clinically acceptable p95: <350ms is OK for triage flows; <250ms preferred for clinician-facing summary screens.

4) Cost target
- We are aiming for an all-in production cost <= $350 / 1M tokens. For pilot we can accept a bit higher while we tune batching and caching.

We'll confirm the CSV with exact request-length histograms by Tue 5/19. Also, happy to take your PoA draft and iterate.

Best,
Samir
Head of Product, Nimbus Health
samir@NimbusHealth.com


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 08:22:00 -07:00
Subject: internal: quick notes on config knobs for Nimbus POC

Ava / Monica — internal quick hit list before we send the tuned PoA: 

- Model choice: recommend a 13B-ish open variant with 8k context to balance latency vs cost. Will provide comparisons in the pricing sheet.
- Batching window: start with 20ms continuous batching on dedicated nodes; if KV hit rate > 30% we can tighten to 12–15ms.
- Cache policy: prefix-level KV caching for 30% multi-turn; expire 30m by default.
- Autoscale policy: target CPU/GPU usage 60–70% for steady capacity, with immediate burst to shared hosted for +30–50% spikes (keeps reserved cost smaller).
- Instrumentation: add token-level cost breakdown in console and a per-route p95 heatmap.

I'll attach a quick sim (separate doc) with cost per 1M tokens across batch sizes 8/16/32 for their token-length profile.

-ben
Ben Carter
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Samir Rao <samir@NimbusHealth.com>
Cc: Elena Park <elena.park@nimbushealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 09:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: scope & budget confirmation — batching & concurrency assumptions

Samir — great, thanks for the clarifications. Confirming next steps and proposed PoC plan: 

1) PoC scope (3 weeks)- Week 0–1: ingest sample traffic (you provide histograms CSV) + verify prefix reuse and KV hit rates.- Week 2: tune batch window and autoscale thresholds; run 48-hour production-like soak to measure p95 and token-unit cost.- Week 3: present final econ and recommended Dedicated sizing + cost per 1M tokens.

2) Technical targets- Target pilot p95: <= 350ms; goal <= 300ms for clinician UI flows.- Expected tuned unit-econ: $320–$380 / 1M tokens with Dedicated + continuous batching (final depends on batch hit rates and average seq length).

3) Scheduling- Please send the token histogram CSV by Tue 5/19.- Tech sync: Wed 5/20 11:00 PT (I sent a calendar invite).

If that all sounds reasonable we'll finalize the PoA and send the DocuSign for a small reserved pilot commit to lock in the rate.

Thanks everyone — looking forward to the tech sync.

Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
(415) 555-0124

title: Findly pilot: relevancy / reranker pilot checklist & next steps
preview:
From: Claire Huang <claire@findly.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@findly.com>
Date: Tue, 12 May 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Relevancy pilot — notes + data ask

Ava / team — thanks for the demo this morning. Quick recap from our side and a few asks so your engs can scope the pilot: 

- Goal: replace heuristic reordering in our search results with a lightweight reranker for top 50 candidates; target improvement: +10–15% NDCG@10 and measurable reduction in user re-clicks.
- Data: we can provide ~2.5M query -> candidate lists (JSONL) with click labels for a rolling 90d window. Average candidates per query ~120, median 48.
- Security: we need an overview of how Redwood handles PII in logs, where embeddings are stored, and whether keys can be scoped to a VPC/private deployment.
- Integration asks (high level): SDK vs HTTP direct; auth options; support for structured output / tool-calling if we want to attach reranker signals back into our orchestration.

Attached: Findly_Rank_POC_requirements.pdf — sketch of data schema and acceptance criteria.

Next steps: could you share a short checklist of what your eng team will need (auth, sample ingestion path, routing for candidate generation vs rerank call, eval harness)? Also copying infra (infra@findly.com) — they had a couple of low-level questions on allowed IPs for dedicated endpoints.

Thanks,
Claire Huang
Head of Search, Findly

---


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Claire Huang <claire@findly.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 12 May 2026 11:00:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Relevancy pilot — notes + data ask

Hi Claire — great call, appreciate the detail. High-level replies and an initial engineer checklist below; Ben/rishi/kim will scope and follow up with a proposed timeline.

Quick answers:
- Pembedding storage / PII: we support customer-controlled key management (KMS) for hosted and Private deployments; we can provide SOC 2 evidence and a short whitepaper on retention controls.
- Auth: API keys for quick POC, JWT/OIDC for integrations, and mTLS available for Dedicated/Private — we’ll include sample config.
- SDK vs HTTP: our SDK wraps retry/backoff, streaming, and structured output parsing; direct HTTP is fine for simple calls but SDK will speed up dev time.

Engineer checklist (what we’ll need from Findly):
1) Sample JSONL (2k rows) with candidate lists + click labels for offline eval.
2) Volume estimate (QPS) and peak token-length expectations.
3) Which fields you want returned with structured output / metadata (e.g., reranker_score, provenance_id).
4) Allowed IP ranges for any dedicated endpoints (if infra needs IP allowlisting).
5) Contact for infra + SRE for network details.

Ben/Rishi — would you pick this up and propose a pilot runbook (scoping, infra, eval metric baseline) by EOD Thu? Kimberly — can you propose acceptance criteria aligned to Findly’s product KPIs?

Regards,
Ava Chen
Enterprise Sales — Redwood Inference

> Ava / team — thanks for the demo this morning. Quick recap from our side and a few asks so your engs can scope the pilot: ...

---


From: Claire Huang <claire@findly.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Findly Infra <infra@findly.com>, Sam Patel <sam.patel@findly.com>
Date: Wed, 13 May 2026 08:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Relevancy pilot — notes + data ask

Thanks Ava — infra contact (copied) is infra@findly.com. A couple of clarifications from our engineers: 

- Data size confirmation: the 2.5M queries is correct; we can export a 2k-row sample for you by EOD.
- Candidate generation: we currently return ~120 candidates from our retrieval step; for the pilot we plan to keep the same generator and call a reranker for top 50.
- Evaluation metrics: we will compute offline MRR, NDCG@10, and also run pairwise preference judgments for a ~500-query human eval tranche. Curious whether Redwood has recommended baselines and tooling for computing these (NDCG variants, bootstrap CI, A/B gating).
- Top_k behavior: we expect to test k in {10, 20, 50} and measure latency/cost tradeoffs; what's a typical latency impact for your neural reranker at top50?

Claire

On Tue, May 12, 2026 at 11:00 Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Claire — great call, appreciate the detail. High-level replies and an initial engineer checklist below; Ben/rishi/kim will scope and follow up with a proposed timeline.

---


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Claire Huang <claire@findly.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 13 May 2026 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Relevancy pilot — notes + data ask

Claire / team — looping in a short technical plan and recommended defaults from the Redwood side. I'll attach a short playbook.

Top-level architecture (recommended):
- Candidate-gen service (Findly) => pass top-50 candidates to Redwood Reranker endpoint.
- Option A (fastest to POC): direct HTTP POST to Redwood ranking API, using API key scoped to a project; SDK not required but recommended.
- Option B (production): SDK + JWT/OIDC + regional routing to a Dedicated pool (if you need consistent latency).

Auth and networking:
- POC: API key in header (x-api-key). We log only metadata (request size/tokens); payload retention can be turned off per team.
- Production: OIDC tokens with short TTL + audience-based routing; mTLS for Dedicated endpoints.
- IP allowlist: we can provide stable egress IPs for Dedicated; for Hosted we recommend using region-based routing and fallback.

Reranker performance notes:
- At top_k=10: add ~8–12ms per request per token on an A100-like pod with our optimized kernels (depends on sequence length).
- At top_k=50: expect 3–4x increase in per-call compute vs top_k=10; batching helps (we can batch multiple rerank calls if QPS justifies).
- Cost estimate: we’ll model cost based on average candidate length and request rate; Ben will share a simple cost sheet.

Evaluation and metrics:
- Offline: recommend computing NDCG@10, MRR, and hit-rate@k. Use bootstrap CIs (1k resamples) for significance.
- Online: canary A/B with Bayesian bandit or fixed-split; we recommend starting with a 5% traffic canary for 2 weeks with predefined success criteria.

Suggested pilot run (milestones):
1) Week 0–1: sample ingest + offline baseline (Findly provides 2k sample)
2) Week 2: deploy POC reranker (top50) + offline eval & tuning (k sweep)
3) Week 3–4: 5% canary with telemetry (latency, NDCG delta, re-click rate)

Attachments: redwood_rerank_playbook.md (short ops checklist); I'll share the cost sheet separately.

Ben Carter
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com

On Wed, May 13, 2026 at 08:28 Claire Huang <claire@findly.com> wrote:
> Thanks Ava — infra contact (copied) is infra@findly.com. A couple of clarifications from our engineers: ...

---


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 14 May 2026 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Relevancy pilot — notes + data ask

Forwarding my quick internal notes after reading Claire's infra questions and Ben's plan.

- Action items for this week:
  * Ben: upload redwood_rerank_playbook.md and cost_estimate.xlsx to the shared Drive link and flag any assumptions.
  * Ava: confirm PO and legal contact for a short POC MSA (we'll aim for a 30d eval window).
  * Kimberly: draft acceptance criteria mapping to NDCG delta / re-click reduction and add guardrails for latency increase (no more than +60ms p50).
- Pilot timeline: Ben to spin up a POC endpoint (hosted) by Mon next week; we’ll run offline eval and propose k sweep (10/20/50).

Small note on metrics: for the initial human eval tranche we recommend at least 500 queries with pairwise judgments; that will give a reasonable signal when combined with offline NDCG and online canary.

Rishi Malhotra
Senior ML Infra, Redwood Inference

- Candidate generation: we currently return ~120 candidates from our retrieval step; for the pilot we plan to keep the same generator and call a reranker for top 50.

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Claire Huang <claire@findly.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 13 May 2026 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Relevancy pilot — notes + data ask

Claire / team — looping in a short technical plan and recommended defaults from the Redwood side. I'll attach a short playbook.

Top-level architecture (recommended):
- Candidate-gen service (Findly) => pass top-50 candidates to Redwood Reranker endpoint.
- Option A (fastest to POC): direct HTTP POST to Redwood ranking API, using API key scoped to a project; SDK not required but recommended.
- Option B (production): SDK + JWT/OIDC + regional routing to a Dedicated pool (if you need consistent latency).

Auth and networking:
- POC: API key in header (x-api-key). We log only metadata (request size/tokens); payload retention can be turned off per team.
- Production: OIDC tokens with short TTL + audience-based routing; mTLS for Dedicated endpoints.
- IP allowlist: we can provide stable egress IPs for Dedicated; for Hosted we recommend using region-based routing and fallback.

Reranker performance notes:
- At top_k=10: add ~8–12ms per request per token on an A100-like pod with our optimized kernels (depends on sequence length).
- At top_k=50: expect 3–4x increase in per-call compute vs top_k=10; batching helps (we can batch multiple rerank calls if QPS justifies).
- Cost estimate: we’ll model cost based on average candidate length and request rate; Ben will share a simple cost sheet.

Evaluation and metrics:
- Offline: recommend computing NDCG@10, MRR, and hit-rate@k. Use bootstrap CIs (1k resamples) for significance.
- Online: canary A/B with Bayesian bandit or fixed-split; we recommend starting with a 5% traffic canary for 2 weeks with predefined success criteria.

Suggested pilot run (milestones):
1) Week 0–1: sample ingest + offline baseline (Findly provides 2k sample)
2) Week 2: deploy POC reranker (top50) + offline eval & tuning (k sweep)
3) Week 3–4: 5% canary with telemetry (latency, NDCG delta, re-click rate)

Attachments: redwood_rerank_playbook.md (short ops checklist); I'll share the cost sheet separately.

Ben Carter
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com

Hi Ethan / team,

Thanks again for the call this morning — quick recap and proposed next steps so we have a single source of truth. Please review and confirm owners where noted.

High-level objective:
- Validate that Redwood Inference can meet Stellar Financial's latency & throughput needs for your batch scoring and nearline chat assistant use cases, while maintaining auditability and data residency controls.

Proposed success criteria (short list):
1) Performance: average 95th pctl latency for chat < 250ms for 512-token requests in our staging traffic shape.
2) Throughput: sustained 1.5M tokens/day during peak batch windows without >5% error rate.
3) Quality: top-line prompt eval set F1 within 6% of baseline model (as agreed in eval doc).
4) Compliance: data ingestion and audit logs for trial are retained for 90 days and accessible to Stellar security team.

Evaluation plan & timeline (draft):
- Week 0 (this week): finalize SOW & RACI. (ACTION: Ethan confirm procurement contact)
- Week 1: infra onboarding + private VPC network handshake (Redwood infra lead + Stellar cloud ops)
- Weeks 2–3: sample data ingestion, prefix cache validation, baseline evals
- Weeks 4–5: stress/throughput run, latency tuning & quantization checks
- Week 6: handoff readout + decision (go/no-go)

Owner asks (proposed RACI entries):
- Infra (VPC, networking, capacity): Redwood Dedicated eng — OWNER: Ben Carter (Redwood) / RACI: Ben - R/A, Connor - C, Ethan - I
- App integration (client SDKs, ingestion pipeline): Stellar app eng — OWNER: Ethan Cole / RACI: Ethan - R/A, Kimberly - C, Connor - C
- Security & compliance (data residency, audit logs, evidence): Stellar security — OWNER: Sophia Martinez / RACI: Sophia - R/A, Mina - C, Ava - I
- Procurement / contracts: Stellar procurement — OWNER: procurement@stellarfin.com / RACI: procurement - R/A, Ben - C

title: RE: April metering discrepancy — token sampling/aggregation question
preview:
From: Ivy Chen <ivy.chen@stormscale.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: billing@stormscale.ai
Date: 2026-04-18T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Discrepancy in April metering — token sampling?

Hi Alex,

Hope you’re well. We pulled the April invoice and noticed that the billed token count is ~18% lower than the totals our instrumentation recorded for the same billing window. I attached a short CSV (usage_sample.csv) with a few request IDs and timestamps and the metering-report.pdf exported from our system.

Questions:
- Has Redwood changed the sampling/aggregation window or enabled any new prefix dedup / cache-related adjustments in the last 30 days?
- Could our migration on 2026-03-31 (we spun up a rollout service) have affected how requests are reported to you — we saw a burst that day and want to rule out misattribution.

This affects our month-end reconciliation and the invoice is currently in our AP queue. We’re happy to provide signed access for a request-level audit if needed. Can you confirm next steps and expected turnaround?

Thanks,
Ivy Chen
Billing & Ops
StormScale Inc.
(415) 555-0123

Attachment: usage_sample.csv (text/csv)
Attachment: metering-report.pdf (application/pdf)


From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Ivy Chen <ivy.chen@stormscale.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, billing@stormscale.ai
Date: 2026-04-18T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Discrepancy in April metering — token sampling?

Ivy — thanks for the note and the attachments, I’ve started a quick triage.

Short answer: no intentional change to billing logic in April that would systematically lower billed tokens for production requests. We did roll a prefix-KV cache optimization to select internal models earlier in April, but that is additive (reduces billed tokens for repeated prefixes) and would not cause unexplained increases in your side’s totals.

Next steps I propose:
1) Loop in Ben from Finance/Accounts so we can coordinate an audit run (Ben — can you pick this up?).
2) If you can sign the data-access consent (simple form), we’ll pull request-level logs for the IDs Ivy provided and compare our ingest events to your events.
3) Quick sync — 30 minutes should be enough once we have the authorization.

Ivy: I attached a short checklist of what we normally need (redwood_prelim_checklist.pdf) to speed the process. If you can confirm you authorize us to pull per-request logs for the IDs in usage_sample.csv, we’ll start a forensics export.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Head of Account Success
Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai | (650) 555-0199

> On 2026-04-18 09:12, Ivy Chen wrote:
> > Hope you’re well. We pulled the April invoice and noticed that the billed token count is ~18% lower than the totals our instrumentation recorded for the same billing window.
> > ...


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Ivy Chen <ivy.chen@stormscale.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: billing@stormscale.ai
Date: 2026-04-19T11:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Discrepancy in April metering — token sampling?

Hi Ivy, Alex — looping in from Finance/Measurements team. I dug into April’s rollout notes and our metering pipeline:

What I can confirm up front:
- No retroactive change to token counting rules (we count input+output tokens per request).
- On 2026-04-01 we enabled an optional prefix KV-caching optimization on a subset of model routes. That optimization reduces billed tokens only when identical prefixes and cached KV usage occur. It cannot create "extra" local tokens on your side.

Potential causes to validate:
- Double-emitted requests during your 2026-03-31 migration (retries or a proxy misconfiguration) could cause StormScale to record more client-side entries than our ingestion saw (or vice versa).
- If your instrumentation aggregates tokens with a different boundary (e.g., counting tokenization before any gateway dedup), totals will differ. We need to align sample request IDs and verify event timestamps.

Proposed approach:
1) We’ll need explicit authorization to run a request-level audit over the April window for the sample IDs. Please use the attached data-access-request-form.pdf — return with a signed initial scope and we’ll export logs within 3 business days.
2) If we find our ingestion missed requests that you recorded, we’ll propose a goodwill credit for confirmed missed ingestion events.
3) If the difference stems from differing tokenization boundaries or local duplication on your side, we’ll share a mapping document to align counting rules going forward.

Ivy — are you available for a 30-min troubleshooting call tomorrow (2026-04-20) at 10:00 PT? Alex — I’ll take point on the export once we have the signed scope.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Operations
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

Attachment: data-access-request-form.pdf (application/pdf)

> On 2026-04-18 10:03, Alex Martinez wrote:
> > Ivy — thanks for the note and the attachments, I’ve started a quick triage.
> > ...


From: Ivy Chen <ivy.chen@stormscale.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: billing@stormscale.ai
Date: 2026-04-22T14:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Discrepancy in April metering — token sampling? — authorization attached

Ben / Alex,

Thanks — attached is the signed consent and the DocuSign link for our AP manager to authorize the audit (docusign-stormscale-consent.pdf). We also re-ran our local sample and found that during the 2026-03-31 migration window a proxy misconfiguration caused a small set of requests to be retried at the gateway level before reaching our application. That produced duplicate entries in our internal instrumentation for about ~0.9% of sampled requests.

We still see ~17% remaining discrepancy after accounting for duplicates, so please proceed with the export. If your audit shows missed ingestion on Redwood’s side, we will expect an invoice credit for confirmed missed events. If it turns out to be counting boundary differences, let’s get a short doc with the canonical counting rules so we can align for May onwards.

I’m available tomorrow for the 30-min call at 10:00 PT.

Best,
Ivy Chen
Billing & Ops
StormScale Inc.

Attachment: docusign-stormscale-consent.pdf (application/pdf)

> On 2026-04-19 11:30, Ben Carter wrote:
> > Hi Ivy, Alex — looping in from Finance/Measurements team. I dug into April’s rollout notes and our metering pipeline:
> > ...

title: Pilot credit allocation and trial extension request - NovaForge
preview:
From: Samir Gupta <samir@launchpad.co>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Morales <elena@foundrylabs.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Apr 2026 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot support request: NovaForge evaluation and trial extension

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the quick product demo last week — the team at NovaForge (we're an accelerator cohort company) is excited about Redwood's latency and cost controls. We're preparing a 6-week evaluation across two internal apps and would like to request: 1) a 60-day trial extension on the hosted plan, and 2) a $10,000 promotional credit to cover pilot usage and a small production-like load.

High level:
- Goal: validate inference cost at 2k TPS over short burst loads, evaluate model variants, and run our prompt-eval suite.
- Timeline: start in early May; run for ~6 weeks.
- Key success metrics: < 250ms median latency and < $0.02/token average cost for baseline prompts.

I've attached our pilot plan and a short usage sketch. Can you let me know what information you need from us and the typical approval cadence for programs like this? If helpful, Elena from Foundry Labs (copied) can vouch for the accelerator process and timing.

Thanks,
Samir Gupta
LaunchPad Ventures (portfolio rep for NovaForge)
samir@launchpad.co
+1 415-555-0123

Attachment: NovaForge-Pilot-Plan.pdf (PDF, 1.2 MB)

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Samir Gupta <samir@launchpad.co>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Apr 2026 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot support request: NovaForge evaluation and trial extension

Hi Samir — really appreciate the clear plan and the context from Elena.

Short answer: we can usually support a pilot extension + credits for accelerator-backed evaluations. To scope it correctly, can you share an estimated token volume per day (or peak QPS) and whether NovaForge needs dedicated capacity or hosted API? Also: are any of the workloads PII-sensitive (if so we can route to a Private trial).

If you can fill this short form it will speed the review: https://redwood.app/pilot-request-form (1–2 mins).

Looping in Ben Carter on sales and Lauren Bishop from Customer Success to help land the offer and MOU. Ben — do you have a customary credit band and conditional terms you prefer for accelerator pilots?

Next steps I suggest:
- Samir completes the form + usage estimate (I attached a small spreadsheet we use)
- Ben/CS propose a conditional credit allocation and milestone success criteria
- We issue a pilot MOU (DocuSign) and enable the extension once signed

Thanks — I'll track this in HubSpot and mark as P1 for accelerator pipeline.

Best,
Ava
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwood.com | (415) 555-0199

Attachment: NovaForge-usage-estimate.xlsx (XLSX, stub)

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Pilot support request: NovaForge evaluation and trial extension

Ava / Lauren —

I reviewed the pilot plan and usage sketch. Recommendation for a clean, low-friction handoff:
- Offer $8,000 in promotional credits (vs. $10k requested) tied to a 60-day extension on the hosted plan.
- Conditions: single-account usage, usage cap of 50M tokens total for the pilot, and sign the pilot MOU within 10 business days.
- Success Milestone: if NovaForge hits the latency and cost targets within the 6-week run, we convert to a Standard trial with optional Dedicated evaluation (SLA terms to be discussed).

Rationale: the requested $10k is fine but $8k keeps our average program burn reasonable for cohort-backed pilots while still covering robust evaluation. If Foundry wants to underwrite any overages we can do $10k and note sponsor contribution.

I'll create the HubSpot deal (DEAL-2026-0147) and draft the MOU. Lauren — can CS prepare a short onboarding checklist and a workspace for testing?

Ben Carter
Sales, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com

Related: https://app.redwood.com/hubspot/deals/DEAL-2026-0147
Attachment: Pilot-MOU-Draft.pdf (PDF, stub)

Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>

Good news: we've approved a conditional promotional allocation for NovaForge. Summary below (officials in HubSpot and MOU to follow):

- Promotional credits: $8,000 USD applied to NovaForge account

- Usage cap: 50,000,000 tokens (pilot aggregate)
- Conditions: signed pilot MOU (DocuSign) within 10 business days; adherence to single-account usage; normal security review (no PII in prompts unless Private trial requested)

Next steps
1) Samir / NovaForge sign the MOU: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1aPilotMOUlink (we'll also route via DocuSign)
2) Lauren will schedule a 30-min onboarding call to provision test keys and a workspace (Lauren: please propose times)
3) Ben will log the deal and mark the promotional credit schedule in HubSpot (DEAL-2026-0147)

If Foundry prefers to sponsor additional overage coverage, Ben can take that in the deal notes and we can top to $10k. Otherwise, we'll start with $8k to get them through the evaluation.

Samir — does this work for NovaForge? If yes, I'll send the DocuSign packet and activate the extension as soon as the signed MOU returns.

Thanks all — looking forward to getting them started.

Best,
Ava Chen

title: Dedicated capacity options, surge lanes & phased activation for Theracore
preview:
From: Sofia Ramos <s.ramos@theracore.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 10 Apr 2026 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Dedicated capacity options — initial constraints

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for the demo last week. We’re narrowing in on dedicated capacity for Theracore’s inference workloads (diagnostic model inference + realtime summarization for clinicians). Before we pull procurement in, can you send the concrete options you typically propose for cluster sizing, minimum GPU commitments, and a realistic ramp schedule?

A few hard constraints on our side:
- We need HIPAA-compliant deployment and an SLA with a 99.9% uptime target.
- Initial budget guidance is tight for Q2 — we could commit to an annual contract if the economics make sense.
- We expect spiky traffic around NPI launches (30–40% daily burst windows).

Specific asks:
1) 3–4 capacity options with estimated monthly committed spend and burst/buffer approach
2) Minimum guaranteed GPUs and recommended warmup/validation windows
3) Onboarding / ramp timeline (how quickly you can activate, warm caches, and reach steady throughput)

Can you get something over by early next week? Procurement will want a clear path (numbers + timeline).

Best,
Sofia Ramos
Head of Cloud Ops, Theracore

---
Attachment: request-constraints-theracore.pdf (1 page)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sofia Ramos <s.ramos@theracore.ai>
Cc: Theracore Procurement <procurement@theracore.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2026 09:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity options — initial constraints

Hi Sofia — thanks for the clear constraints. Attached is a short options pack and an onboarding worksheet that maps cluster sizing to ramp cadence. High-level summary below; full numbers and pricing in the PDF and workbook attached.

Overview (three recommended paths):

1) "Conservative Start" — Proof-of-value lane
- Commitment: 16 GPUs (NVIDIA A800-equivalent slots)
- Ramp: activation in 7–10 days, steady-state by week 3
- Burst approach: small cross-customer burst pool + soft-queueing, up to 2x short bursts
- Typical monthly committed spend (est): $26k–$30k
- When to pick: limited budget, want low risk and fast validation

2) "Standard Production" — balanced throughput + economics
- Commitment: 64 GPUs baseline (can be licensed as 48 min guarantee + auto-scale to 64)
- Ramp: staged activation — 48 GPUs live day 1, 64 by day 21 (full traffic by day 30)
- Burst approach: dedicated burst lanes (reserved but billed on use) + cache warmup credits
- Typical monthly committed spend (est): $95k–$110k (volume pricing available on annual commit)
- When to pick: production SLAs, predictable latency, and cost-efficiency at scale

3) "Enterprise Resilience" — high-availability + geo-redundancy
- Commitment: 128 GPUs with cross-region failover and separate warm pools
- Ramp: 6–8 week full activation (can phase regionally)
- Burst approach: guaranteed burst capacity + priority routing and fallback model variants
- Typical monthly committed spend (est): $180k+
- When to pick: enterprise SLAs, regulatory isolation, large spike windows

Key contract terms and operational notes:
- Minimum guaranteed GPU clause: we recommend a 30-day min guarantee for the first tranche with a 30% ramp tolerance window to accommodate onboarding fluctuations.
- Warmup credits: included (equivalent to ~48 hours of peak throughput) to pre-fill KV caches and avoid cold-start latencies.
- Activation tasks: canary rollout, perf validation runbook, and auto-fallback policy to a compatible lower-capacity model for safety.
- SLA: 99.9% target available on Dedicated offers; enterprise SLA addendum for 99.95% with additional observability retainer.

Attachments: Theracore_Dedicated_Options_20260416.pdf (proposals + pricing), Theracore_Onramp_Worksheet.xlsx (activation schedule + test plan)

Next steps: if you like the "Standard Production" path, I can draft a tailored commitment with a 10% volume discount for an annual contract and propose an activation calendar. Open to a short call with procurement + engineering to walk the workbook.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aditya@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0182

---
Attachment: Theracore_Dedicated_Options_20260416.pdf (3 pages)
Attachment: Theracore_Onramp_Worksheet.xlsx (6 tabs)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Dedicated capacity options — Theracore (need guidance)

Monica/Ben — forwarding Sofia's ask and my proposed options. A few internal points I want your read on before I send the commercial:

- Customer signals they will consider annual if economics are right. Can we offer 10% off list on an annual 64-GPU commit? If so, term sheet should include a 30-day step-down protection (they can ramp down once after month 3 with a 60-day notice).
- Minimum guarantee: customer asked about safety for spikes. I think we should propose a 48-GPU minimum guarantee for month 1, moving to 64 by day 21 — that balances activation risk and shows commitment. Thoughts?
- Warmup credits: include ~48 hours of credits and allow them to run perf validation without billing. Make sure SRE can run the KV-prefill script. Monica, can you confirm the estimated warmup runtime for Theracore's sample traffic?
- DocuSign: if procurement is ready, I want to route a short form (commit + SLA) via DocuSign. Ben, need an expedited quote id and hubspot deal update.

Suggested commercial hook: 10% discount on annual commitment + one month of warmup credits + 2-week activation SLA for the 48->64 phased ramp. We can reserve burst lanes for Q4 product launch with an option to pre-purchase burst credits.

Please reply with edits inline — I want to finalize and send to Sofia tomorrow morning.

Thanks,
Aditya

title: Re: Pilot success metrics & evaluation plan for NovaPay
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@novapay.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-04-12T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Pilot success metrics & evaluation plan follow-up

Alex — thanks for the time this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps from our side so we have a shared acceptance bar for the pilot. Please review and flag anything you want adjusted.

Summary of scope
- Feature: real-time customer dispute summarization and suggested first-response snippets (integration to NovaPay support UI).
- Duration: 3 weeks active evaluation + 1 week validation (4 weeks total).
- Traffic pattern for test: replay of 10k anonymized support threads (provided by NovaPay), plus 100 live-monitoring requests/day for latency sampling.

Proposed success criteria (objective metrics)
- Latency: p95 end-to-end < 250 ms for single-request summarization under pilot throughput.
- Throughput: sustained 60 req/s for a 5-minute window (burst to 120 req/s) with <1% error rate.
- Cost: average cost/token <= $0.0006 (estimated token mix attached).
- Quality: automated QA using your gold set — 85% pass on rubric (coverage, factuality, actionable suggestion). Manual audit on 200 samples: target >= 88% accept.
- Safety: hallucination rate <2% on audited samples; no PII leakage in outputs.

Operational items / ownership
- NovaPay data owner & POC: Alex Johnson (will assign Sofia as engineering POC).
- Redwood PoC lead: Aditya Rao; Eng support: Ben Carter; Success manager: Marissa Cole.
- Deliverables: evaluation dataset onboarding, test harness (we provide), latency/throughput runbook, weekly syncs.

Timeline (proposed)
- Week 0 (this week): NDA/SOW sign, dataset handoff sample (1k rows).
- Week 1: ingest & baseline runs, initial latency profile, make tuning recommendations.
- Week 2: throughput stress runs + batch tuning; begin quality evaluations.
- Week 3: finalize quality tuning & run acceptance tests.
- Week 4: validation window and acceptance decision.

Next steps (actionable)
1) Alex — confirm dataset delivery timeline and point person for sanitized export.
2) Alex — confirm acceptance threshold adjustments (if any).
3) Redwood — send SOW draft, security checklist (SOC2/retention), and a small sample eval set we will use.

Attachments: latency-throughput-plan.xlsx (test matrix), novapay-eval-dataset-sample.csv (30 rows), novapay-pilot-sow-v1.pdf (draft).

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Sales — Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192



From: Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@novapay.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Sofia Martins <sofia.martins@novapay.com>
Date: 2026-04-12T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot success metrics & evaluation plan follow-up

Aditya — this is a great summary, thanks. A few clarifications from our side:

1) Throughput: we actually expect peak support hours to require ~100 req/s sustained for the pilot window. Can Redwood confirm platform can sustain 100 req/s with p95 under 300 ms? If so we can accept the latency target at p95 <300 ms given the higher throughput.

2) Quality: our gold set will be 500 labeled threads (we'll share a 50-row sample first). For manual audit please plan for random sampling of 300 items rather than 200.

3) Security & compliance: procurement will need SOC2 Type 2 and our vendor assessment completed before production rollout. For the pilot we need a short security attestation and a data retention plan for the test dataset.

I've added Sofia (engineer) to own the dataset export and point her to Ben for integration details. Procurement will loop in their channel separately.

If Redwood can push a tightened SOW (with 100 req/s target and the alternative latency band) today, Sofia and I will review and aim to hand off the sanitized dataset by EOD Friday (2026-04-16).

Best,
Alex
Head of Support Automation — NovaPay
alex.johnson@novapay.com



From: procurement <procurement@novapay.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@novapay.com>
Date: 2026-04-13T08:21:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Vendor onboarding checklist / SOW request

Forwarding our vendor onboarding requirements. Aditya — procurement needs:
- Completed vendor security questionnaire (we can start with your SOC2 summary).
- Data retention policy for pilot artifacts (log retention, dataset deletion window).
- Template SOW with pricing caps and termination clauses (pilot-specific).

Can you send the required docs and indicate an expected procurement lead time if SOW is signed this week? We typically take 7–10 business days for standard review; expedited review possible with legal signoff.

Thanks,
NovaPay Procurement Team
procurement@novapay.com

text:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@novapay.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-04-12T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Pilot success metrics & evaluation plan follow-up

Alex — thanks for the time this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps from our side so we have a shared acceptance bar for the pilot. Please review and flag anything you want adjusted.

Summary of scope
- Feature: real-time customer dispute summarization and suggested first-response snippets (integration to NovaPay support UI).
- Duration: 3 weeks active evaluation + 1 week validation (4 weeks total).
- Traffic pattern for test: replay of 10k anonymized support threads (provided by NovaPay), plus 100 live-monitoring requests/day for latency sampling.

Proposed success criteria (objective metrics)
- Latency: p95 end-to-end < 250 ms for single-request summarization under pilot throughput.
- Throughput: sustained 60 req/s for a 5-minute window (burst to 120 req/s) with <1% error rate.
- Cost: average cost/token <= $0.0006 (estimated token mix attached).
- Quality: automated QA using your gold set — 85% pass on rubric (coverage, factuality, actionable suggestion). Manual audit on 200 samples: target >= 88% accept.
- Safety: hallucination rate <2% on audited samples; no PII leakage in outputs.

Operational items / ownership
- NovaPay data owner & POC: Alex Johnson (will assign Sofia as engineering POC).
- Redwood PoC lead: Aditya Rao; Eng support: Ben Carter; Success manager: Marissa Cole.
- Deliverables: evaluation dataset onboarding, test harness (we provide), latency/throughput runbook, weekly syncs.

Timeline (proposed)
- Week 0 (this week): NDA/SOW sign, dataset handoff sample (1k rows).
- Week 1: ingest & baseline runs, initial latency profile, make tuning recommendations.
- Week 2: throughput stress runs + batch tuning; begin quality evaluations.
- Week 3: finalize quality tuning & run acceptance tests.
- Week 4: validation window and acceptance decision.

Next steps (actionable)
1) Alex — confirm dataset delivery timeline and point person for sanitized export.
2) Alex — confirm acceptance threshold adjustments (if any).
3) Redwood — send SOW draft, security checklist (SOC2/retention), and a small sample eval set we will use.

Attachments: latency-throughput-plan.xlsx (test matrix), novapay-eval-dataset-sample.csv (30 rows), novapay-pilot-sow-v1.pdf (draft).

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Sales — Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192



From: Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@novapay.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Sofia Martins <sofia.martins@novapay.com>
Date: 2026-04-12T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot success metrics & evaluation plan follow-up

Aditya — this is a great summary, thanks. A few clarifications from our side:

1) Throughput: we actually expect peak support hours to require ~100 req/s sustained for the pilot window. Can Redwood confirm platform can sustain 100 req/s with p95 under 300 ms? If so we can accept the latency target at p95 <300 ms given the higher throughput.

2) Quality: our gold set will be 500 labeled threads (we'll share a 50-row sample first). For manual audit please plan for random sampling of 300 items rather than 200.

3) Security & compliance: procurement will need SOC2 Type 2 and our vendor assessment completed before production rollout. For the pilot we need a short security attestation and a data retention plan for the test dataset.

I've added Sofia (engineer) to own the dataset export and point her to Ben for integration details. Procurement will loop in their channel separately.

If Redwood can push a tightened SOW (with 100 req/s target and the alternative latency band) today, Sofia and I will review and aim to hand off the sanitized dataset by EOD Friday (2026-04-16).

Best,
Alex
Head of Support Automation — NovaPay
alex.johnson@novapay.com

Forwarding our vendor onboarding requirements. Aditya — procurement needs:
- Completed vendor security questionnaire (we can start with your SOC2 summary).
- Data retention policy for pilot artifacts (log retention, dataset deletion window).
- Template SOW with pricing caps and termination clauses (pilot-specific).

Can you send the required docs and indicate an expected procurement lead time if SOW is signed this week? We typically take 7–10 business days for standard review; expedited review possible with legal signoff.

Thanks,
NovaPay Procurement Team

title: Follow-up: Proposed 'Commuter Bands' for Multi-TZ Deep Dive
preview:
From: Soojin Lee <soojin@redwood.com>
To: Alex Murray <amurray@novacorp.com>
Cc: Priya Singh <priya.singh@novacorp.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2026 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: Follow-up: Proposed 'Commuter Bands' for Multi-TZ Deep Dive

Hi Alex / Priya —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap: Novacorp is prioritizing a live technical deep dive to validate inference throughput for your mixed-traffic (short chat + long-form summarization) use cases, and you asked for a schedule that rotates across US/EU/APAC-friendly times so product teams in each region can join periodically.

Rather than a single fixed time, we often run a 3-band "Commuter Bands" approach that alternates who gets the most convenient slot on a weekly basis. It looks like this (example):

- Band A (US-friendly): Tue 10:00 PT / 13:00 ET / Wed 02:00 SGT
- Band B (EU-friendly): Wed 15:00 CET / 09:00 ET / Thu 00:00 SGT
- Band C (APAC-friendly): Thu 08:00 SGT / Wed 20:00 CET / Wed 11:00 PT

Benefits: equitable engineer attendance, predictable calendar footprint, and better overlap for cross-region Q&A.

Request: please indicate which of the three bands you prefer for the first session next week and who from your infra/product team needs to attend. I attached a draft mutual action plan (NOVA-Mutual-Action-Plan.xlsx) and an .ics with the three sample windows.

If it's easier, here's a Calendly link with the three slots pre-populated: https://calendly.com/redwood-inference/technical-deep-dive — pick a band and we'll lock in a recurring cadence (weekly or biweekly).

Next steps on our side: I'll loop in Ben (sales engineering) to align on the demo scope and confirm a small dataset we can load beforehand.

Thanks — look forward to syncing.

Best,
Soojin Lee
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
soojin@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0198

Attachments: NOVA-Mutual-Action-Plan.xlsx (spreadsheet, 45 KB), commuter-band-availability.ics (calendar file)


From: Alex Murray <amurray@novacorp.com>

Cc: Priya Singh <priya.singh@novacorp.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2026 08:06:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Follow-up: Proposed 'Commuter Bands' for Multi-TZ Deep Dive

Thanks, Soojin —

Appreciate the clear options. Priya and I reviewed the MAP and the bands. For the kickoff next week we prefer Band B (EU-friendly) because our infra folks in London and Berlin can attend more reliably on Wednesdays.

Attendees from Novacorp: Alex Murray (platform lead), Priya Singh (product manager), Daniel Kwon (SRE) — Daniel is in Seoul so he can join at the late-night overlap occasionally but will likely be present for Band C sessions.

One small ask: can your demo include a short run showing tail-latency distribution for small (1–2 token) spammy requests vs longer summary requests? That will help Daniel validate the cache behavior.

I'll pick a slot via the Calendly link later today.

Best,
Alex

Ben — looping you in so you can pair with Daniel on the tail-latency validation and confirm the small dataset (+ synthetic spammy request set) we'll upload ahead of time. Suggested pre-load window: 24 hours before the session so we can run a few warmup passes.

Alex / Priya — a couple of quick operational notes:
- We can demonstrate per-token latency percentiles, KV cache hit-rates, and a short live compare across quantized vs fp16 variants.

title: SaaSFlow: slab pricing simulation & commit phasing
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Walters <ethan@saasflow.io>
Date: Thu, 12 Mar 2026 14:22:00 -0700
Subject: SaaSFlow: slab pricing simulation & commit phasing

Ethan —

Thanks again for the deep dive today. Per our conversation I attached a couple of slab simulations so you can see the unit economics across different commit shapes. The attached slab_simulation_v2.xlsx includes:
- Monthly token bands (0-500M, 500M-2B, 2B+)
- Per-token rate at each band and effective blended rates for 3 commit sizes
- A model for 6-month ramp (0% month 1 -> 100% month 6) and a 12-month steady commit

Key assumptions called out in the sheet: throughput smoothing, auto-burst cap (25%), and prefix cache savings (estimated 12%).

A few things to highlight up front:
- We can offer a slab discount starting at 15% off hosted baseline for a ~1B token/mo commit.
- For multi-month phasing we commonly do a weighted effective credit that preserves margin while acknowledging ramp risk (I included a sample 'phased credit' column).
- Rollover credits are available for unused committed tokens within the quarter (not unlimited).

Can you confirm the monthly token target you expect at steady state and whether your finance team requires invoice-level credits or line-item adjustments? If you want, I can run a simulation using your actual last-12-month token profile — and Ben (pricing) and Laura (finance) are cc'd to help finalize.

Next step: if you confirm the steady-state target and preferred phasing (6 vs 12 months), I'll pull a term-sheet + phased slab proposal.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.com | +1 415 555 0192
Redwood Inference

Attachment: slab_simulation_v2.xlsx (Excel worksheet)


From: Ethan Walters <ethan@saasflow.io>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: SaaSFlow Procurement <procurements@saasflow.io>
Date: Thu, 12 Mar 2026 17:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SaaSFlow: slab pricing simulation & commit phasing

Aditya —

Thanks for sending this over. A few clarifying items from our side:
- Steady-state target: ~1.2B tokens/month (after ramp).
- Preferred ramp: 6 months (we expect ~20% month-over-month growth to reach steady-state).
- Rollover: we need at least partial rollover for unused committed tokens between months within the quarter (Finance prefers line-item credits on the next invoice).
- Billing cadence: prefer monthly invoices with an explicit credit line; open to Net30.

Would also like to see a version of the simulation where the slab is phased (e.g., lower initial minimum with step-ups) and one where we get an annual prepay discount option. Can Ben and Laura loop in suggested legal/billing language for credits?

Ethan

ethan@saasflow.io | +1 206 555 0148
