title: Pricing Exhibit: guardband mechanics and packaging handoff - redline proposal
preview:
From: Jordan Miles <jordan.miles@stratumio.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Rao <procurement@stratumio.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Sep 2025 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Pricing exhibit redline & packaging Qs

Hi Ava —

Thanks again for walking us through the demo. Procurement has run a first pass on the pricing exhibit and returned a few redlines that block internal approval. Their two core asks are: 1) clearer definition of the \"guardband\" / usage floor mechanics so it doesn't trigger unexpectedly during spikes, and 2) clearer language on how partner/marketplace discounts flow through to committed usage calculations.

I've attached the PDF with their markups (StratumIO_PricingExhibit_v3_redline.pdf) and a short table with example math. Can your team confirm:

- whether we can add a 90-day smoothing period for usage spikes before a floor true-up is assessed, and
- whether marketplace partner discounts are applied pre- or post-commit accounting.

Quick call would help — propose Tue 9/16 10am PT?

Thanks,
Jordan
StratumIO — Sr. Procurement Manager
[Attachment: StratumIO_PricingExhibit_v3_redline.pdf (PDF, 243KB)]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Miles <jordan.miles@stratumio.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 11 Sep 2025 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pricing exhibit redline & packaging Qs

Jordan —

Appreciate the detailed notes and the example table. We can do a 90-day smoothing window for true-ups, but legal wants that expressed as a \"smoothing period\" with an explicit maximum adjustment per quarter to avoid retroactive large reconciliations. Suggested language from our side:

- \"Smoothing period: Utilization variance will be assessed over a rolling 90-day window. True-up adjustments will not exceed 25% of the average quarterly committed volume in any single reconciliation.\"

On marketplace discounts: we typically apply partner discounts at the invoice line (post-rate) but count committed-equivalent usage on a pre-discount basis for the purposes of caps/floors so both parties have predictable commitments. That addresses revenue recognition complexity for buyers and avoids double-counting discounts.

Cc'ing Ben/Marissa/Karthik to review potential commercial concessions (credits/buffer) if Stratum needs a lower up-front guard. Also propose we capture the final guardband formula as an explicit table in the Order Schedule to avoid interpretation drift.

Happy to take the Tue 10am slot if that still works.

Best,
Ava Chen
Director, Sales — Redwood Inference
[Signature]


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 12 Sep 2025 08:47:00 -0700
Subject: internal: proposed commercial concession for Stratum (guardband + packaging)

Team —

Read Jordan's notes and Ava's response. Commercially I can offer a one-quarter waiver of the floor for the first billing quarter (i.e., no floor true-up applied for Q1 post-commit), plus a temporary 10% onboarding credit to offset migration variance. This keeps revenue expectation but reduces the customer's risk during ramp.

Key tradeoffs:
- Waiver delays realization of committed revenue (internal impact on ARR smoothing).
- Credits should be capped and expire after 90 days.

If legal agrees to Ava's \"25% cap on adjustments per reconciliation\" language, this combo should be acceptable. I've attached a short spreadsheet with the concession math and an illustration of the invoice lines.

-Ben
Sr. Revenue Manager, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: OrderSchedule_Summary_Table.xlsx (XLSX, 18KB)]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 11:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: internal: proposed commercial concession for Stratum (guardband + packaging)

All —

Legal edit suggestions inline to avoid ambiguity. Major items:

1) Define guardband trigger event explicitly: \"Guardband triggers when sustained utilization falls below 60% of committed monthly utilization measured over a 90-day rolling window (i.e., three consecutive 30-day periods).\"
2) Add an annual true-up cap: \"Annual reconciliation adjustments related to guardband true-ups shall not reduce billed amounts by more than 40% of the annual committed amount in any 12-month period.\"
3) Packaging/discount flow: \"Marketplace partner discounts shall be applied at invoice issuance; however, for the purpose of measuring compliance with committed usage, usage shall be measured on a gross basis (pre-discount).\"

Marissa suggests replacing the term \"smoothing period\" with \"rolling window\" to match standard contract language. Laura — please review the annual cap number; I set 40% as a negotiation anchor but open to lowering.

I'll convert these points to tracked changes in the attached PDF and push back if procurement asks for a full removal of guardband.

-Marissa
Head of Legal Operations, Redwood Inference


From: Jordan Miles <jordan.miles@stratumio.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Rao <procurement@stratumio.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Sep 2025 16:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pricing exhibit redline & packaging Qs — call notes and next steps

Ava/Marissa/Ben —

Thanks for the call earlier. Quick recap and next steps from Stratum:

- The 90-day rolling window + 25% per-reconciliation cap (as described by Ava) is a workable starting point for our CFO, but they asked to replace the 25% cap with a graduated matrix: 0–30 days = 0% adjustment, 31–90 days = up to 25%, >90 days = up to 40% (they want stronger protection for multi-quarter underutilization).
- On discounts, confirmed Stratum prefers counting committed usage on a net basis (post-discount) because partner rebates flow through the billing provider and affect cash flow. They asked if Redwood can run a middle-ground: count pre-discount for the cap/floor math but offer a quarterly reconciliation credit equal to the delta between pre- and post-discount accounting up to a capped amount.

Action items we agreed on:
1) Redwood to send a redline PDF reflecting Marissa's suggested legal edits plus two commercial concession options (Ben's Q1 waiver + onboarding credit vs. a graduated matrix alternative).
2) Stratum to circulate the revised redline to finance and return final position by 9/25.
3) Redwood to prepare a short one-page example worksheet that shows how pre/post discount counting and the quarterly delta-credit would work in practice.

Jordan — please confirm receipt and I will roll these edits into the DocuSign test envelope once we lock the language.

Best,
Jordan
Sr. Procurement Manager, StratumIO
[Attachment referenced: StratumIO_PricingExhibit_v3_redline.pdf]

Subject: Solution Hub: lead registration & margin protection (pilot terms)

Hi Aisha,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — we liked the connector and think there’s concrete demand from two of our mutual customers. Before we lock in a pilot, two operational items we want to align on so we can protect both sides during the initial 90-day window:

1) Lead registration window: Redwood would like a 120-day registration window from the first qualified introduction (MQL -> SAL handoff) where SolutionHub is credited for top-of-funnel introductions. We propose a simple claim form and a 2-week validation turnaround.
2) Margin protection: For the pilot (first 6 months) we can offer a 14% reseller discount baseline but we need a carveout that protects E2E margin if SolutionHub drives bundled implementation services — specifically a cap on retroactive clawbacks unless there’s evidence of dual-sourced sales.

I’ve attached a brief MAP outline (SolutionHub-MAP-v1.pdf) and our onboarding checklist. Can your GTM/legal lead review and confirm these are acceptable or propose concrete redlines?

Next steps: if you’re good with the high-level mechanics we’ll route this through Marissa for final commercial sign-off and legal for a short SOW addendum.

Thanks,
Jordan Blake\Partner Sales\Redwood Inference\jordan.blake@redwood.ai

title: [LumenWorks] Request: queueing behavior review + temporary credits
preview:
From: Samira Patel <samira.patel@lumenworks.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@lumenworks.com
Date: Mon, 14 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Production impact—queueing spike, request for short-term credit/behavior change

Hi Alex,

We saw a significant increase in tail latency and queuing on our reserved pool during yesterday's batch of customer requests (roughly 18:10–18:40 PDT). Several API calls retried which impacted UX for a portion of our customers.

We understand bursts happen, but our contract references a dedicated throughput target and queueing behavior that should minimize this. Could Redwood help with two things?

1) A short-term credit for the token overage incurred during the incident window (rough estimate attached).
2) A quick rundown of what caused the queuing behavior and whether a temporary priority carveout or headroom change is possible while we stabilize our request shaping.

Attached: prod_request_logs_20250712.csv (request timestamps, latencies, pool id).

We'd like to keep things moving—can we get an initial response today?

Thanks,
Samira Patel
Ops Lead, LumenWorks
procurement@lumenworks.com

--
Attachment: prod_request_logs_20250712.csv (CSV, 84KB)

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Samira Patel <samira.patel@lumenworks.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jul 2025 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Production impact—queueing spike, request for short-term credit/behavior change

Hi Samira—thanks for the note and for sending the logs. I'm sorry you hit that window; I know how disruptive retries are for end users.

Quick summary from our side after an initial look at the CSV: there was a concentrated burst from a handful of downstream clients that caused concurrent inflight tokens to exceed your soft headroom threshold on pool RW-Dedicated-14. That triggered the queueing guard which preserves latency SLOs for the pool but pushes lower-priority inflight into the backlog.

Immediate options we can offer:
- Temporary credit for the incremental tokens during the incident window (we can propose a credit amount once Ben confirms billing calculation).
- A time-limited priority carveout for your application for the next 72 hours (limits apply; requires manager approval).
- Config guidance: short-term request shaping (we can share a snippet to reduce concurrency) and a parameterized autoscale bump if you'd like to accept higher cost for reduced queueing.

I've looped in Ben (billing) and Lauren (server capacity lead) to weigh in on credits and the carveout path. Can you confirm the exact pool id and the ingress keys used during the spike (if you have them), and whether you want the carveout window to be immediate?

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com

--
Attachment referenced: redwood_draft_sla_addendum_v1.docx (Draft language for temporary carveouts)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Cc: Samira Patel <samira.patel@lumenworks.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jul 2025 15:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Production impact—queueing spike, request for short-term credit/behavior change

Alex / Lauren—I've run the numbers against LumenWorks' usage. If we base the credit on the incremental token count during the incident window, I'm comfortable offering a one-time credit equal to 80% of the incremental token spend for the affected window (this is discretionary but keeps precedent minimal).

Notes:
- Credit calculation uses the attached CSV and excludes any obvious client-side retry loops (we flagged a few anomalous patterns).
- For the temporary priority carveout: operationally we can enable a 48-hour soft-priority lane scoped to application key lw-main-01 with immediate effect. It will allow a higher inflight headroom allocation but does consume pooled capacity and could slightly increase tail exposure for other tenants—hence why we recommend it only short-term.

Lauren: can you confirm we have capacity headroom in RW-Dedicated-14 to grant lw-main-01 a 48-hour carveout starting now? If yes I approve.

Samira—if you approve the credit and the 48-hour carveout, we'll prepare an SLA addendum and a DocuSign for signature. I'll include the credit amount in the Doc (estimated $1,900 based on our calc).

-Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

--
Attachment: redwood_draft_sla_addendum_v1.docx (Draft addendum with carveout window, credit clause)

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Samira Patel <samira.patel@lumenworks.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Jul 2025 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Production impact—queueing spike, request for short-term credit/behavior change

Confirming capacity: RW-Dedicated-14 has sufficient transient headroom to support a 48-hour scoped priority lane for lw-main-01 starting immediately. Operations will enable the carveout at 11:30 PDT today; monitoring hooks will be in place and we'll rollback if cross-tenant impact is observed.

Ben: please proceed with the one-time credit DocuSign for $1,900 and include the carveout language in the SLA addendum. Samira—we'll send the DocuSign link shortly and will turn on the carveout when you confirm acceptance.

Alex: please move the thread to procurement & legal for quick signature; include the HubSpot deal link and the DocuSign.

Thanks,
Lauren Bishop
Director, Capacity Operations, Redwood Inference
lauren.bishop@redwood.com

--
Action items: enable 48-hour carveout at 11:30 PDT; generate DocuSign with $1,900 credit; attach redwood_draft_sla_addendum_v1.docx.

title: Seed-stage onboarding subsidy & usage rebate — Semaphore Labs
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Park <olivia@semaphorelabs.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Seed-stage onboarding subsidy & usage rebate — Semaphore Labs

Hi Olivia,

Thanks again for the demo last week — loved how you framed the latency requirements for the mobile use case. Per our conversation, I wanted to put a concise proposal on the table for a seed-stage onboarding package that balances runway protection for Semaphore Labs and gives you room to iterate without immediate cost pressure.

High-level offer (proposal A):
- Onboarding subsidy: $5,000 in platform credits redeemable over the first 6 months
- Early-stage usage rebate: 20% rebate on usage billed in the first 12 months (rebate applied as a credit on monthly invoices) up to $50k billed
- Technical onboarding included (2x 2-hour sessions + priority queue for the first 30 days)
- Standard hosted terms; we can add a Marketplace or Dedicated path later if you scale

Attachments: draft-sla-and-sponsor-addendum.pdf (draft addendum to include subsidy and rebate mechanics)

If that looks reasonable I can: 1) loop in Finance to confirm invoicing flow, and 2) send a DocuSign addendum for a straightforward approval path. If you'd prefer more upfront credits in lieu of the rebate we can trade there — happy to discuss.

Are you available Wednesday for a 30-minute call to finalize?

Best,
Alex Martinez
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
---
Alex Martinez | +1 (415) 555-0142 | alex@redwood.com


From: Olivia Park <olivia@semaphorelabs.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@semaphorelabs.com
Date: Mon, 14 Jul 2025 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Seed-stage onboarding subsidy & usage rebate — Semaphore Labs

Thanks Alex — appreciate the clear proposal. A couple quick questions from our side:

1) Our primary ask is runway: can you push the onboarding subsidy to $8k instead of $5k? That helps us cover the first month of higher dev churn.
2) For the rebate: is that a fixed 20% applied to invoice amounts or is it tiered by consumption? We have some months where spikes are expected.

We are planning an internal review with procurement on Tuesday. If you can send an option set (higher credit / lower rebate vs lower credit / higher rebate) that would speed the decision. Also, can you share the capability-overview.pdf you referenced in the demo deck?

Appreciate it —
Olivia Park
Head of Product
Semaphore Labs
olivia@semaphorelabs.com

From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jul 2025 12:28:00 -0700
Subject: FW: Seed-stage onboarding subsidy & usage rebate — Semaphore Labs

Looping you in — Olivia at Semaphore wants more upfront credits (8k) or a clearer tiered rebate. They have a procurement review Tuesday. Can we decide on an approved option I can send today? Below I sketched three options to present (compact tradeoffs so procurement can pick quickly):

Option 1 — Runway-first
- $8,000 onboarding credit (6 months)
- No additional rebate
- Standard 30-day net invoicing
- Requires procurement sign-off only

Option 2 — Hybrid (preferred by me)
- $4,000 onboarding credit
- Tiered rebate: 25% rebate on monthly invoice dollars up to $25k, 15% rebate between $25k–$50k, cap at $50k (rebates applied monthly as invoice credits)
- Adds one line in addendum about how we calculate spike months (7-day rolling average to prevent gaming)

Option 3 — Rebate-heavy
- $2,500 onboarding credit
- Flat 30% rebate on first $40k billed
- Requires Finance pre-approval for rebate accounting flow

Questions for the group:
- Ben: which option will most likely close fast from a commercial POV?
- Naomi: any accounting or rev-rec issues we need to flag (rebates as contra-revenue vs credit)?
- Irene: legal redlines to the draft addendum I attached — anything that would add a >7 day review cycle?

Attachments: capability-overview.pdf (they asked for the deck section), draft-sla-and-sponsor-addendum.pdf

Thanks —
Alex

---
Ben Carter wrote:
> Ship it. Option 2 is a clean commercial compromise — shows flexibility but keeps us aligned to usage economics.

Naomi Feldman wrote:
> Rebate mechanics need to be explicit in the SOW for rev-rec. I can sign off on tiered if Finance confirms the credit application workflow (apply to next invoice, track as deferred credit).

Irene Choi wrote:
> I can turn the addendum in 48 hours if we stick to standard language for subsidy and rebate mechanics. Anything custom for data residency or indemnities will add time.


From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Park <olivia@semaphorelabs.com>
Cc: procurement@semaphorelabs.com, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Jul 2025 01:45:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Seed-stage onboarding subsidy & usage rebate — Semaphore Labs

Hi Olivia — thanks for the quick feedback and for flagging procurement's timeline. We discussed internally and can make a near-term offer that balances your runway needs and our usage alignment.

Final offer (compromise, Option B variant):
- Onboarding subsidy: $5,000 platform credits (redeemable over first 6 months)
- Tiered rebate: 22% rebate on monthly invoice dollars up to $30k; 12% rebate on the portion between $30k–$60k; rebates applied as credits on the following invoice; rebate cap at $60k for the first 12 months
- Technical onboarding: 2x 2-hour working sessions + two weeks of priority support for incident triage
- Invoicing: Net 30, rebates applied as credits; accounting will track credits as deferred liabilities per standard practice
- Legal: simple addendum only; no custom indemnities or data-residency clauses in this package (we can engage for Private deployment later)

Attachments: addendum-semaphore-draft.docx (DocuSign-ready; includes rebate mechanics and measurement approach — we use a 7-day rolling average to smooth spike months)

Next steps:
1) I can send the DocuSign addendum now for procurement approval.
2) If sign-off is quick, we'll schedule the onboarding session within 7 business days of execution.

If your procurement team prefers the higher upfront credit instead of tiered rebate, we can swap to $7.5k upfront and remove the rebate — let me know which path you prefer.

Happy to join a short call if you want to walk through the invoice/rebate examples.

Best,
Alex Martinez

Redwood Inference

Option 3 — Rebate-heavy

Alex

title: Order form questions: auto-renew, notice windows, and termination for convenience
preview:
From: Samiha Khan <samiha@acmeapps.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Order form questions: renewal & termination

Hi Karthik,

Thanks for sending the draft order form. A couple of legal/procurement items came up on our side that will affect procurement signoff — can you confirm Redwood's position on the following?

1) Auto-renew term: The draft shows a 12-month auto-renew. Our procurement team prefers a rolling 6-month renewal (or a 12-month with a 60-day opt-out). Is Redwood able to accommodate either of these?

2) Notice window for non-renewal: We typically require a 60-day notice to avoid auto-renew. The order form language currently says 30 days. Can Redwood match 60 days?

3) Termination for convenience: Our legal team has asked for a defined wind-down period and an indication of whether any early-exit fees apply if we exercise termination for convenience mid-term.

4) Pilot carve-out: We are running a small pilot that we would like to grandfather — i.e., if we opt to terminate the main order, we'd like the pilot workload to continue for 90 days to allow migration.

I've attached the order form we edited with comments (Acme_OrderForm_Draft.pdf). Can we get Redwood's proposed position/alternative redlines by EOD Thursday? If helpful, happy to hop on a quick call.

Thanks,
Samiha
Samiha Khan
Head of Procurement — Acme Apps
Attachment: Acme_OrderForm_Draft.pdf


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Samiha Khan <samiha@acmeapps.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Order form questions: renewal & termination

Hi Samiha — thanks for the clear notes and the annotated order form.

I'll loop in Legal and Sales Ops to give concrete redline options. High level: Redwood is open to discussing a 60-day non-renew notice; the termination-for-convenience treatment has some internal guardrails (we usually allow TFc but expect a short wind-down and pro-rated billing). I want Legal to propose exact language and any constraints (e.g., minimum notice periods, impact on reserved capacity).

Aditya/Laura/Ben — FYI and please propose one conservative redline and one compromise position we can share with Acme. Samiha — can you confirm whether the pilot site is on a separate order or covered by the same order form? That will determine whether we can grandfather it without formal amendment.

Propose a 30-minute call Wednesday 10am PT if that's possible.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Head of Sales — Redwood Inference


From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Samiha Khan <samiha@acmeapps.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Order form questions: renewal & termination (redline proposals)

Hi all — reviewed Acme's annotated order form and sketched redlines. Attaching: Redwood_MSA_v3_redline.pdf (high level notes below).

Summary of proposed positions (two tracks):

1) Conservative (Legal-preferred)
- Auto-renew: Keep 12-month auto-renew but add mutual written notice requirement 60 days before renewal effective date; customer may opt-out only if all outstanding invoices are settled.
- Notice window: 60 days for non-renewal is acceptable if paired with a minimum early termination notice to allow re-provisioning of dedicated or reserved capacity.
- Termination for convenience (TFC): Allow mutual TFC with 90 days' written notice and pro-rated service credit for unused committed term. Exclude pilot accounts from the TFC carve-out unless explicitly added to an amendment.
- Pilot carve-out: We can agree to grandfather pilot workloads for up to 90 days provided they are low-utilization and do not require dedicated reserved capacity.

2) Compromise (Commercial)
- Auto-renew: Change auto-renew to 6 months for orders under $150k ARR; otherwise 12-month auto-renew with 60-day opt-out.
- Notice window: Accept 60 days across the board.
- TFC: Allow Customer TFC with 60 days' notice plus a termination fee equal to 1 month of average monthly spend for the prior 3 months if terminated mid-term. Redwood to provide transition assistance for 30 days. Pilot workloads would be grandfathered for 90 days on a case-by-case basis.

Practical constraints & ask from Acme:
- If the customer's order uses Redwood Dedicated/reserved GPU capacity, any mid-term TFC is disruptive; we would need at least 90 days to reassign capacity or provide a pro-rata settlement with minimum payment for resource reservation.
- Please confirm whether Acme's pilot is on the same ODF/order line and approximate monthly token usage for the pilot (estimate fine).

Next steps: If Acme prefers the Commercial compromise we'll prepare an amendment to the order form. If Legal prefers the Conservative track we'll push that as the proposed redline. Either way I'll prepare language by EOD Wednesday and propose the 30-minute sync.

Attachment: Redwood_MSA_v3_redline.pdf (summary redline with struck/added language)

Regards,
Aditya Rao
Senior Counsel — Redwood Inference
Attachment: Redwood_MSA_v3_redline.pdf


From: Samiha Khan <samiha@acmeapps.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Nguyen <t.nguyen@acmeapps.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 15:38:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Order form questions: renewal & termination

Thanks, Aditya — that is helpful. Quick answers:

- The pilot is on the same order and currently running on standard hosted capacity (not Dedicated). Monthly estimated spend for the pilot is ~3k USD and token usage is low.
- Procurement will accept the compromise track if Redwood can commit to grandfathering the pilot for 90 days and explicitly waive any termination fee for the pilot during that period.
- For the main order, Acme prefers the 6-month auto-renew for orders <$150k and 60-day non-renew notice. The 1-month termination fee for mid-term TFc is likely acceptable provided we get 30 days of migration assistance.

If you can circulate the commercial compromise redline by Wednesday EOD we can share with our GC and procurement and aim for signature the following week.

Appreciate the quick turnaround — Tom and I are available for the Wednesday 10am PT call.

Best,
Samiha
Head of Procurement — Acme Apps

title: Demo recap + proposed POC plan and acceptance checklist - KestrelTech
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Ortiz <elena.ortiz@kestreltech.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 09 Jun 2025 15:20:00 -0700
Subject: Demo recap + proposed POC plan and acceptance checklist - KestrelTech

Hi Elena —

Thanks again for your time today. Quick recap and a compact POC proposal so we can confirm next steps with engineering and procurement.

Call summary (short)
- Goal: validate Redwood inference for KestrelTech’s document-extraction pipeline (invoice/PO normalization) at 99% label-level accuracy and sub-150ms median token latency for inference at 4 concurrent streams.
- Primary success criteria:
  1) Extraction F1 >= 0.92 on our shared sample (see sample-workload-requests.csv)
  2) End-to-end 90th percentile latency < 220ms; median < 150ms for production-ish batch sizes
  3) Token cost amortized to target ~$0.003 per 1k tokens via batching/kv-cache during POC
- Stakeholders: Elena (Kestrel data lead), Arjun (IT integration), procurement (TBD)

Proposed POC (3-week compact sprint) — high level- Week 0 (pre-flight): exchange sample payloads + access method, sign MSA addendum (if needed).- Week 1 (ingest + baseline): Redwood runs baseline inference on provided samples and shares a short report with accuracy & latency metrics.
- Week 2 (optimizations): apply batching/cache/quantization profile; iterate with Kestrel on prompt templates and mapping rules.
- Week 3 (handoff + runbook): final run results, simple runbook for production handover, and a short exec summary for procurement.

Owners (Redwood): Soojin (SE) — baseline & perf tuning; Marissa (CS) — success criteria, acceptance testing; Aditya (AE) — project coordination + procurement point.

Attachments and next steps- Attached: Kestrel_POC_MAP_v1.pdf (mutual action plan stub) and sample-workload-requests.csv (example payloads).- Can you confirm Arjun’s email (for IT integration) and whether procurement wants a formal SOW or a brief addendum?
- If the timeline looks good, we’ll target a POC kickoff the week of Jun 23 and share the baseline report by Jun 30.

Thanks — short and intentional here so we can lock dates.

— Aditya
Aditya Rao | Account Executive
Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0163


From: Elena Ortiz <elena.ortiz@kestreltech.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Arjun Patel <arjun.patel@kestreltech.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Jun 2025 09:08:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Demo recap + proposed POC plan and acceptance checklist - KestrelTech

Hi Aditya —

Appreciate the concise plan. A few clarifications from our side so we’re all aligned:

1) Success criteria: the extraction F1 target is OK but we need per-field thresholds for invoice number, total amount and tax lines — can your SE provide a quick suggested acceptance table for those fields?
2) Latency: the 150ms median target is acceptable but please include a test with our 2x burst scenario (we have spikes up to 12 concurrent streams for payment runs).
3) Data handling: we need confirmation that logs retained during the POC will be encrypted and removed after handoff — SOC2 controls were discussed but procurement will require a short note.

Arjun is on copy; he’ll confirm the integration endpoint (we prefer an s3 presigned ingest for the POC). Procurement (I’ve looped them after this thread) will advise on contracting format — likely an addendum rather than full SOW given the short duration.

Dates: Jun 23 kickoff looks feasible for us, but we need two weeks’ notice for environment prep.

Can Soojin send the per-field acceptance table and a short runbook excerpt for the burst test plan?

Thanks,
Elena
Elena Ortiz
Head of Data & Automation | KestrelTech
elena.ortiz@kestreltech.com

title: Riverview - tenant-scoped forensic retention exception (SIG/CAIQ evidence)
preview:
From: Sophie Marks <sophie.marks@riverviewhealth.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Cc: legal@riverviewhealth.com
Date: Sat, 07 Jun 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SIG follow-up — request to retain tenant debug traces for 180 days

Hi Aditya,

Thank you again for the demo yesterday. Riverview's security team is working through the SIG and CAIQ attachments you shared. One item has come up that I wanted to surface early:

- Our incident response playbook requires up to 180 days of full debug traces for certain regulated endpoints (PCI-adjacent workflows).
- Redwood's default tenant-level ephemeral debug TTL is 30 days.

Can you confirm whether Riverview can request a tenant-scoped exception to preserve full debug traces for a named tenant (tenant id: riverview-tenant-42) for 180 days, and what evidence you would provide in SIG/CAIQ to support that exception (e.g., encryption-at-rest, access logging, proof-of-delete process, change control)?

We need enough artifactation for our SOC auditors and an initial statement of compensating controls to include in our SIG submission. If a formal exception requires an addendum or SOW line item, please flag that as well.

Appreciate a quick read — we are on a tight audit timeline.

Best,
Sophie
Sophie Marks
Senior InfoSec Manager, Riverview Health
Sophie.Marks@riverviewhealth.com

---
Attachments: Riverview_SIG_Template_v3.xlsx


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Sophie Marks <sophie.marks@riverviewhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 09 Jun 2025 10:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SIG follow-up — request to retain tenant debug traces for 180 days

Hi Sophie,

Thanks for the clear summary and the SIG template — we can support a tenant-scoped carveout for forensic/debug traces, with some caveats:

1) We can extend debug trace retention for an identified tenant to 180 days, stored in an encrypted, access-controlled bucket that is logically scoped to that tenant.
2) Access will be limited to a named ops on-call and a small set of authorized engineers; all access will be logged and immutable audit trails will be available for the requested timeframe.
3) We will document the proof-of-delete procedure and provide a sample proof artifact (hash plus timestamp) after deletion.
4) Exceptions that increase storage/operational cost will be treated as a billing add-on under the Dedicated/Private contract or as an enterprise addendum for hosted customers.

I'm attaching a short design doc and the types of artifacts we can provide for SIG/CAIQ (control IDs, screenshots of audit logs, sample proof-of-delete). Ben and Neha — can you review the proposed artifact list and confirm whether we need legal signoff on the carveout wording?

Sincerely,
Aditya Rao
Customer Solutions — Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwood.com

Attachments: forensic-retention-design.pdf, sample-proof-of-delete.txt


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Jun 2025 08:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SIG follow-up — request to retain tenant debug traces for 180 days

Aditya / Neha — quick notes from infra/security:

- Technical feasibility: confirmed. We have a tenant-scoped retention policy flag and a separate encrypted storage tier. Will require a one-time config change and tagging to ensure retention scope is applied only to riverview-tenant-42.
- Audit evidence: we can export AWS CloudTrail entries, internal access logs (S3 access logs), and a signed deletion manifest when the 180-day window ends.
- Risk: extended retention increases exposure surface; recommend strict RBAC and a customer-signed exception form that names the tenant(s) and authorized viewers.

Neha — can you route legal the suggested exception language and the addendum pointer? I think we should also include a sample CLA (consent & exception) in the artifacts.

Ben
Ben Carter
Sr. Security Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

preview:
From: Samir Patel <samir.patel@dotmetrics.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Cc: DotMetrics Legal <legal@dotmetrics.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Jun 2025 08:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MSA + Order Form — IP and training language

Aditya,

Thanks for sending the draft. Our legal team reviewed the MSA and Order Form and flagged three areas that are blocking procurement: output ownership (Section 3.2), training/data-use (Section 7.1), and the feedback/telemetry license (Section 9).

High level asks from DotMetrics:
- Customer retains title to any Customer Output generated by the service (no assignment to vendor).
- No use of Customer content or outputs for training models without explicit opt-in (we need an express \"training opt-in\" rather than implicit rights).
- Any feedback or telemetry Redwood uses must be aggregated, de-identified, and limited to improving the service; cannot be used for model retraining that produces models used by others unless we agree a separate addendum.

We attached our redlines (DotMetrics_MSA_redlines_20250605.pdf). Procurement also needs a proposed tag for a PO and expects a DocuSign-ready Order Form. Can you route this to your legal and provide a short point-by-point response?

Samir

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.patel@dotmetrics.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Jun 2025 13:18:00 -0700
Subject: RE: MSA + Order Form — proposed clause language and fallback

Team / Samir — attaching proposed clause text (also pasted below) to address DotMetrics' concerns while protecting service operation. I tried to keep changes narrow and operational.

Key points:
- Title vs. License: customer keeps title to Customer Output (we admit they \"own\" the output). Redwood requests a limited license back only to the extent needed to provide the service to that customer and to perform troubleshooting/support. No assignment of title.
- Training / Model Updates: For Hosted accounts, default is: Redwood will NOT use Customer Content or Customer Output to retrain foundation models for third-party use unless Customer elects the \"training opt-in\". For Private (VPC / on-prem) deployments, Redwood will not use any Customer Content or Output for training.
- Feedback & Telemetry: Redwood may collect and use Feedback (as defined) solely in aggregated and de-identified form to improve the Redwood service. We propose language that: (a) prohibits use of Customer-specific Content for training without opt-in, (b) allows aggregated telemetry for internal product improvement, (c) limits retention of de-identified telemetry for retraining use to 12 months unless an express addendum is signed.

Proposed patch (plain text for easy redline):
\"Customer retains title to Customer Output. Subject to Customer's ownership of Customer Output, Customer grants Redwood a limited, non-exclusive, revocable license to store, reproduce, and display Customer Output solely to provide the Services, to perform support and troubleshooting, and to comply with law.

Redwood shall not use Customer Content or Customer Output to train or improve models that are used to provide services to other customers unless Customer has separately provided explicit written opt-in for such training. For Hosted Services, Customer may enable an opt-in toggle in the Console to permit use of anonymized and aggregated data for model improvements; absent affirmative opt-in, Redwood will not include Customer Content or Output in training corpora. For Private Deployments, no Customer Content or Output will be used for training absent a signed addendum.

Redwood may collect Feedback and operational telemetry in de-identified and aggregated form and may use such aggregated data to improve Redwood offerings; provided that Redwood will not re-identify or attempt to reverse engineer Customer Content. Use of aggregated telemetry for any model retraining that would result in models offered to third parties requires a separate written agreement. Redwood agrees to a 12-month limitation on using de-identified telemetry for retraining unless Customer agrees to a longer period in writing.\"

Ask: Samir, is the 12-month window acceptable as a starting point? If not, propose a term. Also note the opt-in toggle will be a console feature for Hosted accounts; ops will capture the choice in our contract metadata.

I'll incorporate feedback and produce a redline-ready doc for DocuSign once we settle the high level.

— Ben
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: proposed_clause_text.txt (text, 4KB)

Date: Fri, 6 Jun 2025 09:46:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MSA + Order Form — feedback on proposed language

Ben, Aditya — thanks for the clear drafting. A couple of practical points from procurement/legal here at DotMetrics:

1) The 12-month limit helps, but we prefer a 6-12 month negotiable window. Can you make that a variable (\"not to exceed 12 months unless agreed\")?
2) We need the opt-in to be auditable for compliance. Can you add a clause that Redwood will maintain an audit trail (console flag + change history) and provide a signed attestation on request?
3) The phrase \"revocable license\" is good, but procurement wants explicit carveouts: no sublicensing to affiliates that would repurpose our outputs.

If you can accept those three, procurement is prepared to move to DocuSign and issue a PO. I'll route an internal approval as soon as we have the next redline.

Samir / Priya

Team / Samir — attaching proposed clause text (also pasted below) to address DotMetrics' concerns while protecting service operation. I tried to keep changes narrow and operational.

Key points:
- Title vs. License: customer keeps title to Customer Output (we admit they \"own\" the output). Redwood requests a limited license back only to the extent needed to provide the service to that customer and to perform troubleshooting/support. No assignment of title.
- Training / Model Updates: For Hosted accounts, default is: Redwood will NOT use Customer Content or Customer Output to retrain foundation models for third-party use unless Customer elects the \"training opt-in\". For Private (VPC / on-prem) deployments, Redwood will not use any Customer Content or Output for training.
- Feedback & Telemetry: Redwood may collect and use Feedback (as defined) solely in aggregated and de-identified form to improve the Redwood service. We propose language that: (a) prohibits use of Customer-specific Content for training without opt-in, (b) allows aggregated telemetry for internal product improvement, (c) limits retention of de-identified telemetry for retraining use to 12 months unless an express addendum is signed.

Proposed patch (plain text for easy redline):
\"Customer retains title to Customer Output. Subject to Customer's ownership of Customer Output, Customer grants Redwood a limited, non-exclusive, revocable license to store, reproduce, and display Customer Output solely to provide the Services, to perform support and troubleshooting, and to comply with law.

Redwood shall not use Customer Content or Customer Output to train or improve models that are used to provide services to other customers unless Customer has separately provided explicit written opt-in for such training. For Hosted Services, Customer may enable an opt-in toggle in the Console to permit use of anonymized and aggregated data for model improvements; absent affirmative opt-in, Redwood will not include Customer Content or Output in training corpora. For Private Deployments, no Customer Content or Output will be used for training absent a signed addendum.

Redwood may collect Feedback and operational telemetry in de-identified and aggregated form and may use such aggregated data to improve Redwood offerings; provided that Redwood will not re-identify or attempt to reverse engineer Customer Content. Use of aggregated telemetry for any model retraining that would result in models offered to third parties requires a separate written agreement. Redwood agrees to a 12-month limitation on using de-identified telemetry for retraining unless Customer agrees to a longer period in writing.\"

Ask: Samir, is the 12-month window acceptable as a starting point? If not, propose a term. Also note the opt-in toggle will be a console feature for Hosted accounts; ops will capture the choice in our contract metadata.

I'll incorporate feedback and produce a redline-ready doc for DocuSign once we settle the high level.

— Ben
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: proposed_clause_text.txt (text, 4KB)

Ben, Aditya — thanks for the clear drafting. A couple of practical points from procurement/legal here at DotMetrics:

1) The 12-month limit helps, but we prefer a 6-12 month negotiable window. Can you make that a variable (\"not to exceed 12 months unless agreed\")?
2) We need the opt-in to be auditable for compliance. Can you add a clause that Redwood will maintain an audit trail (console flag + change history) and provide a signed attestation on request?
3) The phrase \"revocable license\" is good, but procurement wants explicit carveouts: no sublicensing to affiliates that would repurpose our outputs.

If you can accept those three, procurement is prepared to move to DocuSign and issue a PO. I'll route an internal approval as soon as we have the next redline.

Short answers:
- Recommendation: start on Hosted API with a pinned model variant and KV-prefix caching. For ~400k tokens/month with bursty traffic, Hosted keeps the operational risk low and reduces lead time. We can migrate to Dedicated if traffic predictably exceeds 1.2M tokens/month or sustained concurrency > 40rps.
- Dedicated lead time: ~10–14 business days to provision a minimal reserved pool (depends on hardware availability and region).
- Compliance: for US-only datasets we need KMS integration (we can use customer-managed keys), and we should enable audit logging from day 1. Brightline legal will need the SOC2 evidence pack; attach capacity-estimate.xlsx for rough sizing.

Oncall / rotation proposal:
- Weekdays: Monica (primary), Vivek (secondary)
- Weekends (first two weeks after pilot start): Vivek (primary), Monica (backup)
- Infra contact alias: infra-team@redwood.com — this reaches SRE/ops rotation.

Operational notes:
- Suggest we include an early ramp smoke test (small traffic window) before the pilot acceptance test.
- Recommend we enable prefix caching and 512-token chunking in the first 30 days to reduce cost and stabilize latency.

I’ll upload capacity-estimate.xlsx to the Drive folder and work with Monica on the intake checklist. If legal needs the SOC2 extract, ping security@redwood.com and I’ll route it.

title: Orchid AI - SLA uptime anchoring and monthly remedy proposal
preview:
From: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@orchid.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Orchid AI Procurement <procurement@orchid.ai>
Date: Tue, 27 May 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the demo last week — the platform looks great. Procurement reviewed the standard SLA you shared and we have two asks before we can move to PO: 

1) Uptime anchoring. For the first 90 days of the pilot we need a provisional uptime floor at 98.0% (rather than the standard 99.5%) due to the expected burst patterns during onboarding. We understand this is temporary and would expect the baseline to re-anchor at 99.5% after the pilot.

2) Latency carveouts. Our inference workload will use scheduled large-batch jobs overnight. We need explicit carveouts for batched inference windows (daily 0100–0400 PT) so batch-induced tail latency doesn’t trigger credits.

Procurement also asked for: monthly remedy cap (we’d like a hard cap of 2% of monthly invoice) and a weekly operational report for the pilot period. I attached our redlines (Orchid_SLA_Redlines.pdf).

Can Redwood accept these pilot terms, or propose an alternative that achieves the same operational protection for Orchid?

Best,
Ethan
Ethan Ross
Head of Platform Ops, Orchid AI
ethan.ross@orchid.ai


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@orchid.ai>
Date: Tue, 27 May 2025 10:09:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Hi Ethan — thanks for the clear note and the redlines. Short answers up front: we can accommodate a temporary pilot uptime anchor and define explicit batch windows as carveouts, but we have a few guardrails we need to propose (see below).

Proposed counterpoints:
- Pilot uptime anchor: Redwood can agree to a 98.0% provisional uptime floor for the first 90 days of the pilot, with an automatic reversion to 99.5% after successful validation. We’ll include objective pass/fail criteria tied to your traffic profile to avoid ambiguity.
- Batch latency carveouts: we can exclude scheduled batch windows (0100–0400 PT) from credit calculations provided you flag those windows in advance and they are limited to repeating nightly maintenance/batch jobs (not ad-hoc production spikes).
- Monthly remedy cap: we typically cap monthly credits but not as low as 2% for pilot customers — that would materially limit remedy viability. Proposed alternative: cap monthly remedy to 5% of invoice for the pilot, with an opt-in to lower caps post-production if you require it.
- Operational reporting: we can provide a weekly pilot health digest (uptime/latency token-level breakdowns, incidents, and KV cache hit-rate) during onboarding.

I’m looping in Ben Carter (Sales) and Laura Bennett (Legal) to align on deal mechanics and a compact SLA amendment doc. Attached is a short counter (Redwood_SLA-Counter_v1.docx) illustrating the language we'd like to use. If that looks reasonable we can finalize legal language and get a DocuSign link out this week.

Next steps: please confirm you’re comfortable with the 5% pilot cap alternative and the batch-window requirements, or propose a compromise. Happy to jump on a 30-min call if that helps.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0192


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 27 May 2025 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Ben/Laura — forwarding Ethan's redlines and my proposed counter. Key points for discussion:

- Pilot uptime anchor at 98.0% for 90 days is grantable. We should add explicit validation criteria: e.g., 7-day rolling p95/availability sample across their top-5 routes. If they fail to reach 99.5% after pilot, we escalate to a playbook rather than immediate credit clawbacks.
- Batch carveouts: OK to exclude nightly windows with the condition that Orchid provides a weekly schedule and we reserve right to reclassify repeated daytime spikes as production traffic.
- Remedy cap: Procurement wants 2%. I pushed back and suggested 5%. Laura, can you take a look at the counter doc and advise if we have room to accept 3% as a compromise during pilot? Also confirm whether we should add a clause limiting credit payouts to a maximum cumulative amount over the pilot term.

Quoting Ethan below for context.

-----Original Message-----
From: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@orchid.ai>
Sent: Tue, 27 May 2025 09:12:00 -0700
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Orchid AI Procurement <procurement@orchid.ai>
Subject: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the demo last week — the platform looks great. Procurement reviewed the standard SLA you shared and we have two asks before we can move to PO: ...


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@orchid.ai>
Cc: Orchid AI Procurement <procurement@orchid.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 28 May 2025 15:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Ethan — thanks for reviewing and for the redlines. Laura and Ben reviewed and we propose the following formal counter for the pilot term: 

1) Pilot uptime anchor: Redwood will document a provisional uptime floor of 98.0% for the first 90 days starting on the go-live date. We’ll include objective validation criteria (7-day rolling availability sample across their top-5 routes) to confirm drift back to 99.5%. If Orchid meets validation, baseline reverts automatically; if not, we engage an operational remediation playbook (no automatic credit acceleration).

2) Latency carveouts: Scheduled batch windows (0100–0400 PT) will be excluded from monthly credit calculations provided Orchid supplies a weekly schedule and the windows are limited to recurring batch jobs. Ad-hoc large jobs outside those windows will remain in scope.

3) Monthly remedy cap: For the pilot period only, Redwood proposes a 4% cap on monthly remedy (a midpoint between your 2% ask and our standard exposure). Payouts remain subject to incident validation and remediation offsets. After the pilot, we revert to our standard remedy cap unless mutually amended.

4) Reporting and cadence: Redwood will send a weekly pilot health digest (PDF) and host a weekly 30-min check-in during the 90-day pilot to review anomalies, trending, and any scheduling conflicts.

Cc: Orchid AI Procurement <procurement@orchid.ai>
Date: Tue, 27 May 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the demo last week — the platform looks great. Procurement reviewed the standard SLA you shared and we have two asks before we can move to PO: 

1) Uptime anchoring. For the first 90 days of the pilot we need a provisional uptime floor at 98.0% (rather than the standard 99.5%) due to the expected burst patterns during onboarding. We understand this is temporary and would expect the baseline to re-anchor at 99.5% after the pilot.

2) Latency carveouts. Our inference workload will use scheduled large-batch jobs overnight. We need explicit carveouts for batched inference windows (daily 0100–0400 PT) so batch-induced tail latency doesn’t trigger credits.

Procurement also asked for: monthly remedy cap (we’d like a hard cap of 2% of monthly invoice) and a weekly operational report for the pilot period. I attached our redlines (Orchid_SLA_Redlines.pdf).

Can Redwood accept these pilot terms, or propose an alternative that achieves the same operational protection for Orchid?

Best,
Ethan
Ethan Ross
Head of Platform Ops, Orchid AI
ethan.ross@orchid.ai


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@orchid.ai>
Date: Tue, 27 May 2025 10:09:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Hi Ethan — thanks for the clear note and the redlines. Short answers up front: we can accommodate a temporary pilot uptime anchor and define explicit batch windows as carveouts, but we have a few guardrails we need to propose (see below).

Proposed counterpoints:
- Pilot uptime anchor: Redwood can agree to a 98.0% provisional uptime floor for the first 90 days of the pilot, with an automatic reversion to 99.5% after successful validation. We’ll include objective pass/fail criteria tied to your traffic profile to avoid ambiguity.
- Batch latency carveouts: we can exclude scheduled batch windows (0100–0400 PT) from credit calculations provided you flag those windows in advance and they are limited to repeating nightly maintenance/batch jobs (not ad-hoc production spikes).
- Monthly remedy cap: we typically cap monthly credits but not as low as 2% for pilot customers — that would materially limit remedy viability. Proposed alternative: cap monthly remedy to 5% of invoice for the pilot, with an opt-in to lower caps post-production if you require it.
- Operational reporting: we can provide a weekly pilot health digest (uptime/latency token-level breakdowns, incidents, and KV cache hit-rate) during onboarding.

I’m looping in Ben Carter (Sales) and Laura Bennett (Legal) to align on deal mechanics and a compact SLA amendment doc. Attached is a short counter (Redwood_SLA-Counter_v1.docx) illustrating the language we'd like to use. If that looks reasonable we can finalize legal language and get a DocuSign link out this week.

title: Control plane IAM consolidation & provisioning questions
preview:
From: Maya Ortiz <maya.ortiz@finedge.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: security@finedge.com
Date: 2025-05-04T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Control plane access / SCIM mappings for private deployment

Hi Aisha,

Thanks for taking the call yesterday. We have a few follow-ups after our security review of Redwood Private: specifically around how you recommend we model operator/service accounts, SCIM group -> role mappings, and any minimum SAML assertion attributes you require for the control plane. Our security team wants to avoid manual role provision for >100 operators across multiple GCP projects; SCIM automation is preferred.

Key asks: 
- Can you share a recommended SCIM attribute map (group -> controlplane role) for delegated operators?
- Do you support SCIM patch events vs full sync? Any rate limits?
- For SAML assertions, what attributes are mandatory for provisioning and login (email, name, groups, role claim name?)
- If we want to use a dedicated service account for CI/CD that needs API-only access, do you recommend a specific IAM role pattern?

I've attached our initial proposed matrix (id-provisioning-matrix.xlsx) and a sample SCIM payload we tried (finedge-scim-example.json) — we hit a 409 on the first PATCH call during testing and want to understand expected idempotency rules.

Appreciate any concrete examples we can hand to our infra team.

Thanks,
Maya Ortiz
Security Engineer, FinEdge





From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Maya Ortiz <maya.ortiz@finedge.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-04T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Control plane access / SCIM mappings for private deployment

Maya — thanks for the clear asks and the attachments. Looping in Laura (identity), Ben (private deployments), and Stephanie (security) so we can give you a single consolidated answer. High level answers below; will follow up with a short mapping doc and JSON examples by EOD.

Quick answers up front:
- We recommend group->role mapping for operator onboarding; SCIM patch semantics are supported (we process PATCH with upsert semantics). Avoid sending duplicate PATCHes for the same external id in a short window — we do eventual de-dup with 409 responses as a signal.
- Mandatory SAML attributes for our control plane: email (primary), fullName, and groups. If you want policy-level claims, include an 'roles' claim with the claim name 'x-redwood-roles' (string list).
- For CI/CD service accounts we recommend a minimal API-only token approach: create a tenant-local service principal, map it to a 'service-api' role that only has generate-token and model-invoke permissions. Avoid mapping CI/CD accounts to full operator roles.

Laura — can you produce a concise SCIM attribute map (group -> role) and an example PATCH payload. Ben — any constraints from Dedicated/VPC side on service principal IP allowlists or JWT issuer expectations? Stephanie — can you confirm if we should include a short note about token rotation cadence for FinEdge's compliance team?

Thanks,
Aisha Rahman
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference



From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>, Maya Ortiz <maya.ortiz@finedge.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-04T13:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Control plane access / SCIM mappings for private deployment

Maya — attaching the SCIM mapping excerpt below and a compact PATCH example. Notes inline.

--- SCIM mapping guidance ---
Recommended group name -> Redwood control plane role
- redwood-ops-admins -> controlplane.operator.admin
- redwood-ops-read -> controlplane.operator.readonly
- redwood-model-eng -> controlplane.model.editor
- redwood-service-accounts -> controlplane.service-api

Key attributes we consume from SCIM user object:
- externalId (preferred): used as stable id if present
- userName: mapped to login email if no externalId
- displayName / name.familyName + givenName -> profile fields
- groups[].value (group display name) -> role mapping

PATCH example (simplified):
PATCH /scim/v2/Users/46
{
  "Operations": [
    {
      "op": "replace",
      "path": "groups",
      "value": [ { "value": "redwood-ops-admins" } ]
    }
  ]
}

We handle PATCH as an upsert for group membership; if a group referenced in the patch is unknown to the tenant mapping, we log and return 422 with a helpful message. 409s typically happen when you try to CREATE a user with an externalId that already exists — in your example the first call was a POST and the client retried. Our recommendation: use PATCH for membership updates and POST only for initial user creation when the externalId is absent.

Attachment: id-provisioning-matrix.xlsx (contains the full mapping + attribute expectations)

Hope this helps — I can export a canned example in both Okta and Azure AD SCIM flavors if you want.

Laura Bennett
Principal Identity Engineer
Redwood Inference



From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>, Maya Ortiz <maya.ortiz@finedge.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-05T10:08:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Control plane access / SCIM mappings for private deployment

Maya — quick operational constraints from the Private (VPC/on-prem) side:
- IP allowlists: For our private control plane, operators typically authenticate via SAML SSO through the customer's IdP. We front the control plane with a small set of egress IPs for webhook callbacks; if you need inbound allowlisting for service principals, we support CIDR blocks per-tenant (minimum /28) or mTLS client certs for stronger assurance.
- JWT issuer: If you plan to mint tokens from your own OIDC provider for CI/CD, the control plane accepts any issuer that is configured in the tenant's IdP settings (we validate the issuer, kid, and exp claims). We recommend a unique audience claim 'redwood-controlplane' to avoid cross-tenant token replay.
- SCIM rate limits: 30 PATCH/second per tenant, burstable to 150 for short windows; long-running full syncs should use our bulk import endpoint rather than iterative PATCHes.

If you want, we can provide a terraform example for the network ACLs and a sample mTLS cert rotation playbook.

Ben Carter
Senior Systems Engineer, Private Deployments
Redwood Inference



From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Maya Ortiz <maya.ortiz@finedge.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-06T15:48:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Control plane access / SCIM mappings for private deployment

Maya — consolidating next steps and attachments so your infra & security teams have a single checklist. Please confirm whether you prefer Okta or Azure AD canned examples and whether FinEdge will run a bulk import (recommended for >50 users) or live SCIM sync.

Action items (owner / ETA):
- Laura: provide Okta + Azure AD example PATCH payloads and a small mapping JSON. (ETA: 2025-05-06 EOD)
- Ben: draft a small terraform snippet for tenant CIDR allowlist + mTLS note and provide the minimum /28 CIDR example. (ETA: 2025-05-07)
- Stephanie: include a short note on recommended token rotation cadence and central audit log retention for FinEdge auditors (ETA: 2025-05-07)
- Maya / FinEdge: indicate whether you will run bulk import or keep live SCIM sync (reply to this thread)

Attachments included: id-provisioning-matrix.xlsx, finedge-scim-example.json

If helpful, we can schedule a 30-minute follow-up to walk through a test tenant and run a live PATCH test.

Best,
Aisha

title: Org access charter: ArcadiaHealth pilot - API keys & admin invites
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Sofia Martinez <s.martinez@arcadiahealth.com>, Josh Reynolds <josh.reynolds@arcadiahealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 22 Mar 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Org access charter: ArcadiaHealth pilot - API keys & admin invites

Hi Sofia / Josh —

Great to connect yesterday. As discussed, this thread will capture the kickoff items for the ArcadiaHealth pilot (3-week internal evaluation + one small production route). Goal is to have a working environment with seeded API keys and admin users by the end of next week so your engineers can exercise the integration.

Key asks to start implementation planning:
- Please confirm the pilot project name you want us to create (we'll create a Redwood project and seed API keys scoped to it).
- Provide a short list of 2–3 workspace admins (name + email + role) — attached is a CSV template (arcadia-admin-roster.csv).
- Any constraints on API key expiry, IP allowlists, or regional data residency (if applicable).

What we will deliver before handoff to CS:
- One project created in your org with two admin API keys (one primary, one rollback).
- Seeded roles and permission map based on your roster.
- MCP Onboarding Checklist completed and shared (attached).

Proposed timeline:
1) Today: Receive admin roster + pilot project name.
2) Mon-Wed: We seed the project, create keys, and run a smoke test.
3) Thu: Handoff call with implementation owner and your engineers.

I've attached the onboarding checklist and a draft MPA for legal to review. Monica & Ben — once Sofia confirms the roster, can you own key seeding and schedule the smoke-test window?

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0198

Attachments: arcadia-admin-roster.csv (text/csv), MCP-Onboarding-Checklist.xlsx (spreadsheet), MPA-draft-Arcadia-20250322.pdf (pdf)

From: Sofia Martinez <s.martinez@arcadiahealth.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Cc: Josh Reynolds <josh.reynolds@arcadiahealth.com>
Date: Sat, 22 Mar 2025 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Org access charter: ArcadiaHealth pilot - API keys & admin invites

Thanks Aditya — we liked the plan. A couple quick notes:
- Pilot project name: arcadia-pilot-telemetry

title: Dedicated capacity demo intake — BrightBridge (priority lead)
BluePeak: confirm 'no egress' / disconnected environment assumptions
Helioscope pilot: dedicated capacity sizing and SLO headroom
preview:
From: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sana Rao <sana@helioscope.ai>
Date: Fri, 15 Mar 2024 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Hi Ava —

Thanks for the demo last week. We want to scope a small Dedicated deployment for a July pilot (NDA done). Quick background: our inference pattern is a mix of short chat completions and a reranking endpoint used by our search service. Typical request payloads are ~40–80 tokens; p95 tends to skew towards longer multi-turn flows.

Traffic profile (weekdays):
- Peak concurrent requests (observed): ~60 QPS on search rerank windows (bursty, 30s peaks).
- Average chat QPS outside peaks: 12–18.
- Average tokens per request: 55.
- 95th percentile tokens per request during peak windows: 140.

We care about p95 tail latency <= 300 ms for search rerank (single-turn) and < 700 ms for chat multi-turn flows. Can you help with a rough tokens/sec estimate and how many GPUs we'd need to reserve for Dedicated to meet SLOs with a reasonable headroom buffer? I attached a small CSV with sampled traffic (helioscope_traffic_samples.csv).

Appreciate a short runbook/plan we can show the engineering stakeholders.

Cheers,
Liam
Director of ML Ops
Helioscope AI

Attachments: helioscope_traffic_samples.csv (CSV, 28 KB)

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 15 Mar 2024 11:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Hi Liam —

Thanks — received the CSV and skimmed the burst patterns. Karthik (our deals/solutions lead) and Ben (ops) are looped to help with concrete headroom math. Quick ask: can you confirm the model family you expect to use in pilot (13B vs 70B equivalent) and whether quantized weights are acceptable? That drives per-GPU throughput.

I'll sketch a short, conservative runbook with three scenarios (chat-focused, search-heavy, mixed). Expect a first-pass estimate in the next 24 hours.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference

---
On Fri, Mar 15, 2024 at 9:12 AM Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai> wrote:
> Thanks for the demo last week. We want to scope a small Dedicated deployment for a July pilot (NDA done)...

Attachments: (referenced) helioscope_traffic_samples.csv

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>
Date: Sat, 16 Mar 2024 08:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Team —

I pulled the sample CSV and ran quick throughput math. Assumptions:
- Pilot model: 13B-class quantized (we recommend this for cost/latency tradeoffs unless customer insists on a 70B).
- Measured baseline throughput (our conserved expectation): 600 tokens/sec per A100-80GB equivalent when targeting p95 latency budgets around 250–350 ms for single-turn completions. For multi-turn chat with 140-token requests, expect higher GPU time per request; we model on tokens/sec basis.
- Target SLOs: search rerank p95 <= 300 ms (single-turn), chat p95 <= 700 ms (multi-turn averages).

Calculations (mixed workload):
- Peak burst scenario: 60 concurrent requests in 30s window. Use conservative per-request tokens = 140 (p95). Tokens/sec during peak = 60 * 140 = 8,400 t/s.
- Baseline (non-peak) chat: 18 QPS * 55 tokens = 990 t/s.
- Combined peak + baseline (worst overlap): ~9,390 t/s.

GPU demands:
- At 600 t/s per GPU -> raw GPUs = 9,390 / 600 ≈ 15.65 -> 16 GPUs to cover peak sustained tokens/sec (no headroom).
- Add operational headroom: 25% buffer for short-term surges, cache misses, KV overhead -> 16 * 1.25 = 20 GPUs.
- Warm cache / prefix caching can drop effective GPU usage for chat flows with repeated context; assume 10–20% improvement if caching correctly configured, so effective GPUs might be 16–18 in practice.

SLA/fallback considerations:
- If the customer accepts model quantization and batch fusion, we can push per-GPU to ~750 t/s for short bursts, lowering the required GPUs to ~13 raw -> ~16 with headroom.
- For strict low-latency single-turn rerank, reserve a small pool (2–3 GPUs) pinned to low-latency routing to avoid queueing behind heavy chat batches.
- If they require 70B for quality, per-GPU drops to ~120–200 t/s (depending on optimizations) which would multiply GPU needs by ~3–5x; we should call that out as a non-starter for a small pilot.

Next steps for proposal:
1) Confirm model family and quantization tolerance (ask customer).
2) We produce an appendix with token-distribution curves, conservative headroom, and recommended Dedicated slab purchase: recommend a 20-GPU slab for July pilot with canary-scaled burst (add-on) of +4 GPUs for 90-day window.
3) Provide an ops-runbook: cache warm-up schedule, autoscaling throttle windows, and routing policy for rerank vs chat.

I'll attach a quick workbook (capacity_model_v2.xlsx) with the calculations and a short slide we can send to Helioscope.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Solutions Lead | Redwood Inference

Attachments: capacity_model_v2.xlsx

From: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sana Rao <sana@helioscope.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Mar 2024 09:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Thanks Karthik — this is helpful and in-line with our internal napkin math. A few clarifications:
- We are comfortable with quantized 13B for the pilot.
- We cannot exceed $X/month for Dedicated slab (I put the exact number in the HubSpot note).
- Search rerank must stay within 300 ms p95; if that requires routing to a hot pool that's fine.

Can you call out an options set where we prioritize cost vs latency? Specifically:
A) Min-cost option that still meets SLOs most of the time (best-effort tails)
B) Latency-priority option (strict tail guarantees)

If you can share the workbook and a single-slide comparative table we can show engineering and procurement next week that would be ideal.

Liam
Director of ML Ops, Helioscope AI

---
Attachments: (referenced) helioscope_traffic_samples.csv

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Mar 2024 16:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Liam / team —

Attaching the workbook and a 1-pager (helioscope_mvp_malpresentation.pdf) that lays out two configuration bundles per your ask: Cost-focused and Latency-focused. Summary (short):

Option A — Cost-focused- Reserved slab: 12 GPUs (13B quantized)- Routing: best-effort single pool, opportunistic prefix caching enabled- Expected p95 search latency: ~280–350 ms (tails may spike during sustained 30s bursts)- Monthly cost estimate: (redacted)

Option B — Latency-priority- Reserved slab: 20 GPUs (13B quantized) with a hotpool of 3 GPUs pinned for rerank routing- Routing: policy routes rerank -> hotpool; chat -> shared slab with aggressive caching- Expected p95 search latency: <= 300 ms with <10% chance of tail violations during extreme bursts- Monthly cost estimate: (redacted)

Operational notes:
- We recommend a 14-day warmup before the pilot to prime caches.
- Add a burst add-on (4 GPUs) available for the first 90 days; billing prorated.

I uploaded the finalized workbook to Drive and updated the HubSpot record with the two options. Link below.

Thanks — happy to schedule procurement sync.

Ben Carter
Solutions Ops | Redwood Inference

Attachments: capacity_model_v2.xlsx, helioscope_mvp_malpresentation.pdf

Related links: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2bHelioSizingLink, https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/123456/company/helioscope
